Notably, Girlschool collaborated with Motörhead as 'Headgirl' for the St. Valentine’s Day Massacre EP, also achieving a UK Top 5 position. Their early image focused on leather and denim rather than glam, emphasizing raw, high-energy live performances. Throughout the 1980s, the band adapted their style to evolving trends in heavy metal, including a shift toward a more commercial American FM-friendly sound after lineup changes. Despite facing challenges and a brief hiatus in 1988, Girlschool remained active and influe

item.title: Ghana Immigration Service Trains Officers Ahead of Nationwide E-Visa Rollout
item.description: The Ghana Immigration Service (GIS) is preparing for the nationwide launch of Ghana's electronic visa (e-visa) system, scheduled for May 25, 2026. To ensure smooth implementation, GIS recently conducted a two-day training program in Accra, bringing together 40 officers from various immigration control points across the country. The training focused on equipping officers with the necessary skills to manage the fully digital platform, covering system workflows, document verification, monitoring procedures, and online payment processes. The e-visa system aims to simplify visa applications for foreign nationals, improve passenger processing, enhance security, and facilitate better data collection for national planning and international cooperation. Acting Deputy Comptroller-General Faisal Disu emphasized the importance of discipline, professionalism, data protection, and responsible handling of traveler information. The initiative is expected to promote transparency, accountability, and in

item.title: James Carville Advocates Targeting Republican Party Switchers in the Southern U.S.
item.description: Democratic strategist James Carville has proposed a new initiative aimed at attracting Republicans in the traditionally conservative Southern states to the Democratic Party. Speaking on his Politicon podcast, Carville emphasized that Democrats should actively pursue 'party switchers' in states such as Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, Arkansas, and Texas. He cited recent polling indicating that 15 to 20 percent of Republicans oppose former President Trump, compared to nearly unanimous Democratic support for their party. Carville suggested that Democrats offer significant opportunities to these potential converts, including leadership positions, campaign support, and financial backing, arguing that they could gain more by switching parties than by staying in the GOP. He compared this approach to the strategies Democrats used in the 1970s and 1980s, advocating a proactive effort to rebuild influence in the South by recruiting moderate Republicans dissatisfied with their party. Carville st

item.title: Willy Sluiter: Celebrating the Life and Art of a Dutch Master
item.description: Willy Sluiter (1873–1949) was a distinguished Dutch painter and graphic artist whose work captured the spirit of early 20th-century life with both elegance and authenticity. Born into a well-off family, he developed an acute understanding of societal nuances and became renowned for his lively depictions of both high society and everyday people. Sluiter's paintings often portrayed fashionable figures at popular seaside resorts such as Scheveningen, as well as the vibrant social scenes of The Hague and Rotterdam. At the same time, he was skilled in representing the rugged lives of Katwijk fishermen, demonstrating remarkable versatility across his subjects. Beyond painting, Sluiter was an accomplished poster designer and caricaturist, known for his bold lines, witty observations, and commercial art that remains iconic. His works provide a rich visual record of the Netherlands during a period of cultural transition, combining traditional Dutch artistry with the flair of the Belle Époque an

item.title: Michael Cohen Applies for Compensation from Trump's Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: Michael Cohen, former personal attorney to President Donald Trump and now one of his most vocal critics, is seeking compensation from the newly established $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization' fund created by the Trump administration. The fund aims to compensate individuals who claim to have been targeted by politically motivated actions from federal law enforcement. According to an NBC News report, Cohen argues that he is a prime example of such mistreatment, citing experiences during the Trump presidency including the revocation of his home confinement in retaliation for cooperating with authorities and writing a book critical of Trump. Cohen was convicted of campaign finance violations related to hush money payments to Stormy Daniels and other charges, including lying to Congress. He also claims his tax information was unlawfully leaked by an IRS contractor in 2018, similar to complaints Trump has made about his own tax records. In a letter, Cohen stated that if the fund is intended

item.title: New York Mayor Ensures Affordable World Cup Tickets for Local Residents
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, in collaboration with the NYNJ Host Committee, has secured 1,000 tickets to the 2026 FIFA World Cup for New York residents at the affordable price of $50 each. These tickets are intended to make the event accessible to working-class New Yorkers, a stark contrast to the standard ticket prices, which can reach over $500 for group stage games at MetLife Stadium. Winners of these tickets will also benefit from free bus transportation to and from the stadium, helping reduce the financial burden for local fans. The tickets will be distributed via a lottery system running from May 25 through May 29, and resale of these tickets will not be allowed. The initiative emphasizes inclusivity, allowing children, security personnel, restaurant workers, and other everyday New Yorkers to experience the World Cup firsthand. Mayor Mamdani has previously criticized FIFA for setting exorbitant ticket prices that exclude local fans. This program, supported by NYC World Cup

item.title: NOAA Forecasts Below-Average 2026 Atlantic Hurricane Season Driven by Emerging El Niño
item.description: The 2026 Atlantic hurricane season is expected to be less active than usual, according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). The agency predicts between eight to 14 named tropical systems, with three to six becoming hurricanes and one to three reaching Category 3 or higher. The primary factor behind this outlook is the anticipated emergence of an El Niño event this summer. El Niño, characterized by warmer Pacific waters, alters global weather patterns and typically increases wind shear in the tropical Atlantic, disrupting hurricane formation and intensification. Historical super El Niño years have shown below-normal accumulated cyclone energy. However, other variables remain influential. The Atlantic Ocean is currently warmer than average, which could support storm development, while Saharan dust outbreaks may suppress activity, as observed in the previous year. Despite the lower forecast, experts emphasize that even quiet seasons can produce devastating storms

item.title: Oprah Winfrey's 1971 Miss Fire Prevention Win in Nashville Launched Her Broadcasting Career
item.description: In 1971, a 17-year-old Oprah Winfrey won the Miss Fire Prevention contest in Nashville, Tennessee. As the only Black participant, she stood out not through traditional beauty standards but through her intelligence, ambition, and strong public speaking abilities. The pageant, sponsored by local radio station WVOL and the Nashville Gas Company, emphasized personality and presence over appearance. During the final judging, when asked what they wanted to be when they grew up, other contestants gave conventional answers such as teacher or nurse. Oprah cleverly differentiated herself by stating she aspired to become a television journalist like Barbara Walters. This response helped secure her victory. For winning, she received a Longines watch and a digital clock radio. The win proved pivotal for her future. When Oprah visited WVOL radio station to claim her prizes, a DJ asked her to read news copy on tape. Impressed by her voice and delivery, the station immediately hired her to read aftern

item.title: Joan Collins: Vintage Portraits of the Legendary Actress and Dynasty Star
item.description: Joan Collins, the renowned English actress, author, and columnist, has enjoyed a career spanning over seven decades in the entertainment industry. She first gained prominence during Hollywood's Golden Age in the 1950s and 1960s, known for her striking beauty and commanding screen presence in various films. However, it was her iconic role as the scheming and glamorous Alexis Carrington Colby in the 1980s prime-time soap opera Dynasty that catapulted her to international superstardom. Her performance is widely credited with boosting the show's ratings and establishing the archetype of the stylish, vengeful 'power bitch' character on television. Beyond acting, Collins has authored numerous best-selling novels and lifestyle books, showcasing her versatility as a writer. Her contributions to the arts and charity work earned her the prestigious title of Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire (DBE) in 2015. Even in her later years, she continues to embody timeless elegance and rema

item.title: The SS Matsonia: Celebrated Luxury Ocean Liner of the 1950s
item.description: The SS Matsonia, relaunched in 1957 and prominent by 1959, epitomized luxury travel between California and Hawaii during the post-war era. Originally built in 1932 as the SS Monterey, the ship underwent an extensive $20 million reconstruction to cater to the rising demand for tropical vacations. Its interiors, crafted by renowned designer Harry Neafie, combined mid-century modern elegance with Polynesian-inspired motifs, offering passengers both comfort and style. In 1959, the Matsonia became a floating gateway to paradise, transporting hundreds of tourists across the Pacific while delivering service and glamour that defined the Matson Line’s golden age. Later, in the 1960s, she was renamed the SS Lurline to carry on the prestige of her sister ship. The 1959-era Matsonia remains a cherished symbol of a time when the journey to Honolulu was as luxurious and memorable as the destination itself. Vintage Kodachrome slides capture her voyages, preserving the visual history of mid-century oc

item.title: Trump’s Victory Arch in Washington, D.C. Receives Conditional Approval from Federal Commission
item.description: President Donald Trump received partial approval for his proposed 'victory arch' near the Lincoln Memorial in Washington, D.C., as the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts, largely composed of his appointees, voted to advance the project. While the arch, sometimes nicknamed the 'Arc de Trump,' will move forward, some design elements were scaled back. Vice Chairman James McCrery II recommended removing the lions at the base and canceling plans for an underground pedestrian tunnel, both of which were eliminated from the final design. The arch’s height would remain significant at 250 feet, potentially dominating the skyline and affecting sightlines from the Lincoln Memorial to Arlington National Cemetery, though it is still shorter than the Washington Monument at 555 feet. Preliminary surveys and site testing have already begun, despite reports that the Trump administration plans to proceed without Congress’s approval, citing a century-old authorization for a different monument on the same site.

item.title: Online Communities on 4chan Are Creating Nonconsensual Deepfake Images of Women
item.description: Research has uncovered a troubling trend on 4chan, where anonymous users, often male, are requesting and creating nonconsensual sexualized deepfake images of women. These requests, posted in threads specifically dedicated to 'adult requests,' involve images of women who may be publicly visible on social media. Users request these altered images to depict women undressed, engaged in sexual acts, or in humiliating scenarios. The individuals who manipulate the images are referred to as 'wizards' and are praised for their work, creating a hierarchical dynamic that reinforces misogyny. This activity is not isolated but often connected to personal relationships, including family, colleagues, or acquaintances, making the abuse more targeted. Researchers, including Leonie Oehmig from the Institute for Strategic Dialogue, note that these deepfakes spread beyond 4chan to private platforms like Telegram and Discord. While some claim the content is for personal entertainment, others use it to hara

item.title: Leonie Vernet: Celebrating the Legacy of a French Fashion Icon
item.description: Leonie Vernet (1934–1993) was a defining figure in mid-20th century French fashion, renowned for her elegant presence and chiseled features. As a model for prestigious designers such as Givenchy and Balenciaga, she exemplified the artistry and sophistication of haute couture during the 1950s and 1960s. Vernet's poise and ability to animate architectural garments made her a favorite for runway shows and editorial photography. Captured by legendary photographers like Irving Penn and Henry Clarke, she graced the pages of Vogue and L’Officiel, embodying what became known as 'Parisian Chic.' Her versatility allowed her to showcase everything from tailored suits and luxurious coats to intricate evening gowns, often collaborating closely with designers to bring their creative visions to life. Beyond her beauty, Vernet earned admiration for her professional longevity and her influence as a muse, particularly to Hubert de Givenchy, shaping an era of fashion elegance that continues to inspire de

item.title: GOP Senators Express Strong Opposition to Trump's $1.7 Billion Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: A closed-door meeting between Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche and Senate Republicans on Thursday became highly contentious, centering on former President Donald Trump's proposed $1.7 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund. Reports indicate that the meeting lasted nearly two hours and was described by multiple attendees as chaotic and hostile. Republican senators expressed significant concern over the administration's refusal to guarantee that the funds would not be accessible to individuals involved in violent acts during the January 6 Capitol attack. Sen. Thom Tillis (R-NC) strongly criticized the fund, calling it 'stupid on stilts' and likening it to 'tyranny.' According to several media outlets, most GOP senators voiced opposition, with few defending the measure. Some lawmakers also warned that the controversy surrounding the fund could jeopardize the passage of other priorities, such as a major immigration enforcement and reconciliation bill. Overall, the meeting highlighted g

item.title: Astrid Kirchherr and Stuart Sutcliffe: A Pioneering Love Story in Early Beatles History
item.description: The story of Astrid Kirchherr and Stuart Sutcliffe, often referred to as the “fifth Beatle,” is both romantic and tragic. Their relationship began in 1960 when Astrid, introduced to The Beatles by her then-boyfriend Klaus Voormann, was immediately captivated by Stuart. Despite a language barrier—Astrid spoke little English, and Stuart no German—they formed a deep bond. Stuart became enamored with European art and culture through Astrid and her friends, known as the “Exies,” while she influenced the Beatles’ early visual style, including their iconic haircuts and fashion choices. The couple quickly became engaged, and Stuart even left The Beatles in 1961 to pursue his passion for painting while staying close to Astrid in Hamburg. Their story ended tragically on April 10, 1962, when Stuart died at just 21 from a cerebral hemorrhage, with Astrid by his side. The loss deeply affected both Astrid and John Lennon, who had considered Stuart his close friend and alter ego. Astrid later describ

item.title: Senator Mark Kelly Shows His NASA Spacesuit With a Hidden Feature in His Office
item.description: Senator Mark Kelly, a former NASA astronaut with a 15-year career, has placed the spacesuit he wore on the space shuttle on display in his office. Kelly, an Arizona Democrat, traveled to the International Space Station four times between 1996 and 2011. However, the suit contains an unusual hidden detail that recently drew attention online. Barry Bonzack, who works on NASA's Orion project as part of the Artemis program, shared a video highlighting the surprising feature of Kelly’s suit, cautioning viewers that it is striking and memorable. This moment of humor comes shortly after Kelly shared serious concerns with comedian Hasan Minhaj regarding tense messages he received from world leaders at the Munich Security Conference, indicating some international friction. While Kelly’s display combines both a nod to his NASA career and a lighthearted element, it also reflects his ongoing public presence and ability to connect his unique experiences in space exploration to his political career.

item.title: Remembering Catherine O’Hara: Iconic Actress and Comedic Talent
item.description: Catherine O’Hara, the celebrated Emmy-winning actress known for her roles in Beetlejuice and Home Alone, passed away on January 30, 2026, at 71, at her Brentwood home in Los Angeles. Her agency, Creative Artists Agency, confirmed her death followed a brief illness. While the official cause has not been disclosed, O’Hara had a rare condition called situs inversus, where her internal organs were mirrored. O’Hara’s career was marked by unforgettable performances, including Delia Deetz in Beetlejuice, and her iconic scream of 'Kevin!' in Home Alone, which remain memorable highlights of classic films. Macaulay Culkin, her co-star, paid tribute to her heartfeltly. Notably, O’Hara briefly joined the cast of Saturday Night Live in 1981 but left after one week due to loyalty to her previous show, SCTV, which was renewed unexpectedly. She later hosted SNL twice in the early 1990s. O’Hara’s legacy continues to be celebrated for her comedic genius and influence on film and television, leaving a la

item.title: Photographer Jess Bell Captures Dogs in Vibrant Mid-Air Leaps
item.description: Ontario-based photographer Jess Bell has created a stunning series of images showcasing dogs in mid-air, surrounded by clouds of colorful, non-toxic powder. By dusting the dogs as they leap, Bell achieves a magical, almost surreal effect that highlights the energy and joy of these animals. The series emphasizes the dynamic movement and playful spirit of dogs, capturing moments that feel both dramatic and whimsical. Through her work, Bell conveys not only the beauty of canine motion but also the bond between pets and their owners, as she notes the shared joy in photographing these lively subjects. Published on Moss & Fog, the series has drawn attention for its creativity and unique visual approach, blending photography and artistic expression to celebrate the extraordinary capabilities of our four-legged friends. Each image evokes a sense of wonder, inviting viewers to see dogs in a new, imaginative light.

item.title: Dorothy Malone’s Early Career and Iconic 1940s Film Moments
item.description: Dorothy Malone, born January 29, 1924, began her Hollywood career in the 1940s as a brunette often cast in wholesome or supporting roles. Discovered at age 19 while performing in a college play at Southern Methodist University, Malone spent the decade building her career through B-movies and supporting parts for studios like RKO Pictures and Warner Bros. Her most memorable moment from this era came in the 1946 film noir The Big Sleep, where she played a bespectacled bookstore clerk who revealed a sultry side in a brief scene with Humphrey Bogart. Malone also appeared in films such as The Falcon and the Co-eds (1943) and Westerns like South of St. Louis and Colorado Territory (1949), maintaining her natural brunette hair and showcasing her striking beauty and poise. By the late 1940s, she left Warner Bros. to work as a freelance actress, opening opportunities for more varied roles in the following decade. The article presents a collection of 30 glamorous photos highlighting Malone’s ear

item.title: Audit Finds Millions Misused by Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office in Racial Profiling Settlement
item.description: A decade-long audit of the Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office (MCSO) has revealed that millions of dollars intended for a class-action settlement addressing racial profiling were misused or misattributed. The settlement stemmed from a 2013 federal ruling that found deputies under former Sheriff Joe Arpaio had violated the constitutional rights of Latino drivers. While the county has spent over $353 million on settlement-related reforms since 2013, auditors found nearly 72% of the $226 million examined was improperly charged, including extravagant purchases such as golf carts, cable subscriptions, high-rise office renovations, and Tasers not required by the court. The audit highlighted a lack of oversight from the Maricopa County Board of Supervisors, which rarely questioned the sheriff’s spending. Current Sheriff Jerry Sheridan, along with Republican supervisors, have defended the expenses and sought to end court oversight. Meanwhile, the audit noted that spending on body cameras, staffi

item.title: Historical Reasons Behind the Placement of Buttons and Zippers on Men’s and Women’s Clothing
item.description: The placement of buttons and zippers on men’s and women’s clothing has a long history rooted in tradition and practicality. Historically, wealthy European women wore complicated dresses that often required assistance to fasten. Buttons were placed on the left side of women’s garments so a right-handed servant could easily dress them. Men, who typically dressed themselves, had buttons on the right side to accommodate their dominant hand and practical needs, such as military traditions where men carried swords on their left side. When clothing production moved to factories in the 19th century, these conventions were standardized and continued even as zippers became popular in the early 1900s. Today, many brands still follow these traditional patterns, but modern fashion increasingly embraces unisex and gender-neutral designs, breaking the historical rules. The left-right difference in fasteners is now mainly a vestige of the past rather than a functional necessity, showing how small deta

item.title: SpaceX Discloses Financial Performance Ahead of Potential IPO
item.description: SpaceX has released its financial data for the first time as the company prepares for a possible initial public offering (IPO). According to the filing, the company generated $18.7 billion in revenue in 2025, a 33% increase compared to 2024. In the first quarter of 2026, revenue reached $4.7 billion, up from $4.1 billion during the same period the previous year. Despite this growth, SpaceX reported a loss of $4.9 billion in 2025, a sharp decline from a $791 million profit in 2024. The losses were attributed to a substantial increase in capital expenditures, which nearly doubled to $20.7 billion, driven largely by investments in artificial intelligence development. The first quarter of 2026 mirrored these losses, with SpaceX losing $4.3 billion. The financial disclosures highlight the company's aggressive expansion strategy and heavy investment in emerging technologies. Analysts and investors are debating the implications of these results, particularly considering SpaceX's dominance in

item.title: The 1910 Paige-Detroit Model B Roadster and Its Innovative Two-Cycle Engine
item.description: The 1910 Paige-Detroit Model B Roadster represents an important chapter in the early history of American automobile manufacturing. Introduced by the newly formed Paige-Detroit Motor Car Company, this vehicle stood out for its distinctive three-cylinder, two-cycle engine at a time when most manufacturers favored more conventional four-cycle designs. This mechanical choice highlighted the experimental spirit of the Brass Era, when companies tested various engineering approaches to find the right balance of power, reliability, and cost. The Model B featured a lightweight, open-air chassis typical of roadsters from the period, complemented by large wooden-spoke wheels and prominent brass fittings that gave it a sporty yet elegant appearance appealing to affluent enthusiasts and gentleman drivers of the early 20th century. Its minimalist design emphasized functionality and the thrill of open-road driving. Although the two-cycle engine was eventually discontinued in favor of more standard po

item.title: Dennis Hopper's Photographic Journey Through 1960s America
item.description: Before Dennis Hopper became known as a rebellious icon of New Hollywood and the director of 'Easy Rider,' he faced a period of professional blacklisting after a fallout with director Henry Hathaway. During this time, his wife, Brooke Hayward, gifted him a 28mm Nikon camera, which he carried everywhere throughout the 1960s. Hopper’s photography captured candid, intimate moments of Hollywood stars, influential artists, musicians, and cultural movements with a commitment to realism. Rejecting flash and darkroom cropping, he produced raw, high-contrast black-and-white images that conveyed authenticity and immediacy. His work documented iconic figures such as Paul Newman, Jane Fonda, Andy Warhol, and Tina Turner, while also providing historical glimpses into civil rights marches and Hells Angels gatherings. Hopper’s photographs from 1961 to 1967 offer a vivid visual record of American subcultures and the artistic and political landscapes of the decade. This period of photography informed hi

item.title: Prenatal Exposure to Chlorpyrifos May Cause Lasting Brain Changes and Motor Impairments in Children
item.description: A recent study conducted by researchers from Columbia University's Mailman School of Public Health, Children's Hospital Los Angeles, and the Keck School of Medicine of USC reveals that prenatal exposure to chlorpyrifos, a widely used insecticide, can lead to persistent brain abnormalities and motor deficits in children. The study analyzed 270 children born to African-American and Latino mothers, all of whom had detectable chlorpyrifos levels in their umbilical cord blood. Brain imaging and behavioral assessments conducted between ages 6 and 14 showed a clear dose-dependent relationship: higher prenatal exposure was associated with more pronounced structural and functional brain changes, as well as reduced motor speed and coordination. Although the U.S. EPA banned indoor residential use of chlorpyrifos in 2001, the chemical continues to be applied in agriculture, posing risks to individuals in farming communities. The researchers warn that other organophosphate pesticides may carry simi

item.title: Plant-Based Diet Linked to Reduced Weight Gain in Midlife Women During Menopause
item.description: A new study led by researchers at Harvard Medical School suggests that midlife women can reduce weight gain during menopause by following a plant-based diet. The research monitored 38,283 American nurses, with an average age of 45.6, over 12 years, tracking their diets, physical activity, and health markers. Women following diets rich in fruits, vegetables, legumes, and whole grains, and low in red and processed meats, salt, and ultra-processed foods, gained significantly less weight compared to those with diets high in meat, potatoes, and processed snacks. On average, women in the study gained 1.7 pounds per year, while those adhering to the plant-focused diet gained only 0.6 pounds per year, resulting in about 7.5 pounds less over the study period. The study also highlighted that such diets can reduce the risk of obesity and chronic diseases like type 2 diabetes, heart disease, stroke, and cancer. Researchers emphasized incorporating these dietary strategies into routine midlife care

item.title: Early Detection of Thyroid Cancer in Women: Symptoms, Risks, and Treatments
item.description: Thyroid cancer often develops quietly, with subtle or no early symptoms, making it easy to overlook. Common early signs can include a small lump in the neck, voice changes, or difficulty swallowing, though many lumps are benign. In Malaysia, thyroid cancer is the eighth most common cancer among women, affecting them more than men, though the exact causes remain unclear. Most cases are slow-growing types like papillary or follicular cancer and respond well to treatment when detected early. Standard treatment usually involves surgical removal of part or all of the thyroid, sometimes followed by radioactive iodine therapy to target remaining thyroid cells. Advanced or resistant cases may require targeted therapies such as tyrosine kinase inhibitors. Lifelong follow-up and hormone replacement therapy are critical for patients after surgery. Doctors emphasize that early attention to neck changes dramatically improves outcomes. Since routine thyroid screening is not common in Malaysia, raisi

item.title: A Retrospective Look at Jean Simmons’ Career and Iconic 1950s Portraits
item.description: Jean Simmons (1929–2010) was a celebrated British actress and singer whose career spanned from post-World War II British cinema to major Hollywood productions. Unlike many 1950s starlets, she was known for her expressive eyes and versatile acting, capable of portraying both delicate ingenues and strong, independent women. Notable roles included performing her own vocals in 'Guys and Dolls' (1955) alongside Marlon Brando, starring in Technicolor epics like 'The Robe' (1953), and portraying the femme fatale in the film noir 'Angel Face' (1953). Beyond her on-screen achievements, Simmons faced significant challenges, notably a legal battle to gain independence from Howard Hughes, who attempted to control her career and appearance. This bold move enabled her to choose diverse roles that solidified her legacy. The 1950s marked both her artistic triumphs and personal resilience, making her a memorable figure in cinema history. The vintage black-and-white portraits highlighted in this article

item.title: Fedora and SUSE Remove Deepin Desktop Due to Security Concerns
item.description: Deepin Desktop Environment (DDE), once praised for its aesthetics and potential to revolutionize Linux desktops, has faced persistent security concerns that have led major distributions to drop it. Initially, Deepin faced criticism around 2018 when its Deepin Store transmitted unencrypted data to a Chinese analytics service, which included user browser information. While the core system was found free of spyware, security issues resurfaced in 2025 when SUSE identified policy violations in the way Deepin packages were installed, bypassing standard review procedures. Following SUSE's move, Fedora also decided to remove Deepin packages from its repositories due to similar concerns and poor maintenance response. As a result, users can no longer install Deepin Desktop from official Fedora or SUSE repositories, though building from source remains an option. This development reflects the importance of strict code reviews in open-source projects. Despite its removal, Deepin's design influence

item.title: Acting AG Blanche signs IRS settlement granting Trump family immunity, raising legal and ethical questions
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche has signed documents that grant President Donald Trump and his family immunity from IRS scrutiny, sparking controversy over the legality and ethics of the settlement. The deal also establishes a $1.8 billion compensation fund for Trump allies, which critics have labeled a 'slush fund.' Blanche maintains that he did not personally draft the settlement, suggesting it was prepared by Trump's outside counsel and Justice Department lawyers, though it bears his signature. Legal experts have expressed concern that the arrangement may represent an unprecedented conflation of the president’s personal interests with federal law enforcement, effectively using the Justice Department to shield Trump from potential financial and legal liabilities. Senators, including Chris Van Hollen, have questioned Blanche’s dual role and whether he is acting in the public interest or for Trump’s personal benefit. While Blanche insists his actions are lawful and necessary,

item.title: Bob Uecker and Bob Gibson's 1964 Team Photo Prank and Its Consequences
item.description: In 1964, during the St. Louis Cardinals team photo session, backup catcher Bob Uecker convinced ace pitcher Bob Gibson to hold hands as a lighthearted prank. Both players smiled for the photograph, aiming to ease the tension of the formal moment. However, team management deemed the gesture unprofessional, considering it a distraction. The original photo was discarded, and the team had to pose again for the official image, with Uecker and Gibson separated this time. Both players were fined approximately $200–$300 for their actions. Uecker later recounted the story on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson in 1971, humorously noting that holding Gibson's hand was a strategy to avoid being hit by his pitches. While some observers have interpreted the gesture as a subtle message of racial unity—since Gibson was Black and Uecker white—the players consistently described it as a harmless joke. The incident has since become a memorable anecdote in baseball history. That same season, the Cardi

item.title: Photographic Journey Through the Home Counties of 1950s England
item.description: Hardwicke Knight’s photography offers a captivating glimpse into the Home Counties of England during the 1950s, capturing the post-war era with remarkable clarity and nostalgia. His images document the delicate balance between the region’s lush countryside and the orderly growth of suburban life, highlighting towns in Surrey, Kent, Hertfordshire, and surrounding areas. Knight’s keen eye turns everyday scenes—such as ivy-covered pubs, steam-filled railway stations, and meticulously maintained gardens—into striking works of art that reflect both the charm and quiet resilience of the period. From the scenic Seven Sisters cliffs in Sussex to rural villages in Oxfordshire and Hertfordshire, his photographs preserve the natural and architectural beauty of mid-20th century England. These visual records serve not only as artistic achievements but also as historical documents, showcasing a society in transition, maintaining traditions while slowly embracing modernization. Through Knight’s lens,

item.title: Trump Comments on U.S. Casualties in Conflict with Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump, during a media appearance on a tarmac, spoke about the casualties in the ongoing U.S. conflict with Iran, noting that 13 American service members have died. He compared this figure to previous wars, claiming it was low relative to other conflicts. Trump referenced the U.S. exit from Afghanistan in 2021, where 13 service members died, though he confused the administration responsible, mentioning Obama instead of Biden. He also reiterated previous misstatements, such as attributing the Iran nuclear deal to President Biden rather than former President Obama. The conflict with Iran has resulted in over 3,000 Iranian casualties and prompted Iran to close the Strait of Hormuz, contributing to rising global oil prices and economic concerns. Trump's statements drew attention due to the casual tone in which he described the loss of American lives, highlighting ongoing controversies around his communication style and record on foreign policy. The remarks sparked discussio

item.title: TeamPCP Cybercrime Group Conducts Large-Scale Supply Chain Attacks on Open Source Software
item.description: TeamPCP, a cybercriminal group, has escalated software supply chain attacks to an unprecedented level, compromising hundreds of open source tools used by developers worldwide. Their latest target was GitHub, where a poisoned VSCode extension led to the exposure of nearly 4,000 internal code repositories. TeamPCP has previously breached other organizations, including AI company Anthropic and data contractor Mercor. The group operates by planting malware in widely used developer tools, harvesting credentials, and then distributing malicious updates, creating a self-reinforcing cycle of compromise. They have automated many attacks using a worm called Mini Shai-Hulud, which spreads across networks and encrypts stolen credentials. Financial gain drives TeamPCP, with ransomware and data sales being common tactics, though they also leak data publicly if buyers are not found. Security experts emphasize that organizations can mitigate risk through token management, restricted access, and cautio

item.title: Leucine Enhances Mitochondrial Efficiency and Cellular Energy Production
item.description: Researchers at the University of Cologne have identified the essential amino acid leucine as a key regulator of mitochondrial energy production. Mitochondria, the cell's energy powerhouses, rely on specific proteins to function efficiently. The study revealed that leucine protects these outer mitochondrial membrane proteins from degradation, allowing cells to generate energy more effectively during periods of increased demand. A protein called SEL1L, which normally marks damaged proteins for destruction, is partially suppressed by leucine, further enhancing mitochondrial function. Experiments in roundworms and human lung cancer cells demonstrated that disruptions in leucine metabolism can impair mitochondrial activity and may influence disease processes, including fertility and cancer cell survival. This research highlights how nutrients like leucine actively regulate cellular energy beyond their traditional role in protein synthesis, suggesting potential applications in therapies for

item.title: Trump's Endorsement of Ken Paxton Could Risk Republican Senate Seat in Texas
item.description: Fox News chief political analyst Brit Hume warned that former President Donald Trump's endorsement of Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in the Republican Senate runoff may inadvertently benefit Democrats. Trump’s support for Paxton, following a March primary where no candidate won a majority, comes despite incumbent Senator John Cornyn having backing from Senate leadership figures such as John Thune and the Senate Leadership Fund. Hume suggested that Paxton’s controversial history, including a 2023 impeachment by the Republican-controlled Texas House for alleged abuse of office, could alienate key Republican voters. Additionally, allegations of misconduct and personal scandals could undermine his electability in the general election. Hume described Trump’s rationale for supporting Paxton as “pretty flimsy” and warned that it might weaken Republican control of the Senate by giving Democrat James Talarico a viable opportunity. The Wall Street Journal noted that Trump could be credited if

item.title: Jane Fonda’s Breakout Role in ‘Walk on the Wild Side’ (1962)
item.description: In the 1962 film 'Walk on the Wild Side,' Jane Fonda delivered a powerful early performance as Kitty Twist, a cunning and street-smart drifter who becomes involved in the darker aspects of a New Orleans brothel. Although she was not the main lead, Fonda's portrayal stood out for its depth, combining vulnerability with a sharp, sometimes predatory edge. Her character serves as a stark contrast to the more refined figures of the 'Doll House,' navigating the Southern Gothic atmosphere with raw energy and authenticity. Dressed in worn clothing and sporting a disheveled hairstyle, Fonda fully embodied the rebellious spirit implied by the film’s title. This role marked a critical moment in her transformation from a fresh-faced young actress into a mature performer capable of tackling complex and provocative roles. Through her depiction of Kitty Twist, audiences witnessed Fonda’s early ability to convey both desperation and resilience, signaling her future prominence in Hollywood as a versati

item.title: Evelyn Hofer’s Timeless Fashion Photography from the 1950s and 1960s
item.description: Evelyn Hofer was a pioneering fashion photographer whose work during the 1950s and 1960s distinguished itself through a serene, meticulous approach. Unlike many of her contemporaries, Hofer avoided the high-energy chaos of runway photography, instead using a heavy 4x5 view camera that required patience and precision. Her photographs for major publications like Vogue and Harper's Bazaar emphasized the structure and elegance of garments, treating clothing almost as architectural forms. Hofer’s portraits captured the essence of her models, combining careful composition with painterly qualities. Notable works include fashion spreads featuring Carmen Dell' Orefice, Dorian Leigh, and models wearing pieces by designers such as Carter's, Anne Fogarty, and Lilly Daché. She also photographed jewelry, hairstyles, and beachwear, always with attention to detail, color harmony, and geometric composition. Hofer’s work bridged commercial fashion photography and fine art, resulting in timeless images

item.title: Nvidia Earnings to Signal Future Growth in AI Semiconductor Market
item.description: Nvidia Corp., a leading AI semiconductor company, is set to release its earnings report, which could confirm whether the ongoing rally in chip stocks has room to continue or signal caution for investors. Chip stocks have been strong performers in 2026, but recent market volatility has raised concerns. Wall Street anticipates another strong quarter from Nvidia, driven by continued AI infrastructure investment from major tech companies like Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Meta. Analysts are looking for guidance on future growth, as sustained demand for AI chips could support Nvidia’s expansion into 2027 and beyond. Despite its market leadership, Nvidia faces increasing competition from rivals like AMD and internal chip development by large customers. The stock has risen nearly 36% since late March but is experiencing some short-term volatility. Valuation metrics indicate Nvidia shares are trading at less than 24 times estimated earnings, below the 10-year average, suggesting growth pote

item.title: Vizio Faces Trial Over Linux-Based Smart TV Source Code Access
item.description: The Software Freedom Conservancy (SFC), a nonprofit advocating for open source software, is taking Vizio to court over its refusal to release the full source code for its Linux-based smart TV operating system, Vizio OS. The lawsuit, filed in 2021, claims that Vizio violated the terms of the GNU General Public License (GPLv2) and Lesser GPL (LGPLv2.1), which require companies distributing Linux-based software to provide access to the source code. The trial, set for August 10 in California, could have wider implications for smart TV owners, as many modern TV operating systems are Linux-based. Full access to Vizio OS code would allow users to limit ads, control tracking, and potentially extend the lifespan of their devices. While Vizio has shared partial code, SFC argues it is insufficient to compile executable versions of the OS. Vizio contends that SFC is not a third-party beneficiary of the GPL, and therefore cannot enforce compliance, emphasizing that GPLs are software licenses rather

item.title: Understanding Nonprofit Fraud: Investigations, Oversight, and Donor Risks
item.description:
Recent high-profile prosecutions of nonprofit organizations have brought nonprofit fraud into the spotlight. In Minnesota, federal authorities prosecuted a major COVID-19 pandemic-related fraud case, where several nonprofits allegedly misappropriated approximately $250 million from a federally funded child nutrition program. Defendants faked meal counts and reimbursement claims, spending the money on luxury items. Similarly, the Department of Justice recently indicted the Southern Poverty Law Center for alleged fraud, highlighting increased federal scrutiny of nonprofits, particularly those engaging in politically sensitive activities.
Nonprofit fraud, defined as misuse of assets, remains difficult to quantify compared to corporate or government fraud. The Association of Certified Fraud Examiners estimates nonprofits lose around 5% of annual revenue to fraud, with an average incident costing about $76,000. Nonprofits are less likely than corporations to provide fraud training, leaving

item.title: Former Epstein Assistant Testifies About Ongoing Abuse During His Incarceration
item.description: Sarah Kellen, a former assistant to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, is scheduled to testify before the House Oversight Committee, detailing the abuse she suffered and the control Epstein exerted over her life. According to her opening statement, obtained by legal analyst Lisa Rubin, Kellen experienced sexual abuse from Epstein even while he was jailed. She recounts being forced to undress for him during video calls from the Palm Beach County stockade. Kellen emphasizes that Epstein manipulated influential figures worldwide, attending events with global leaders, including former presidents and royalty, which amplified his power and made it nearly impossible for victims to resist or seek justice. She also highlights that Epstein's legal arrangements, such as the 2008 Florida plea deal, were used to silence co-conspirators and prevent victims from cooperating with law enforcement. Kellen describes her experience as living with a 'permanent virtual reality headset,' underscoring th

item.title: Apple TV’s 'Margo’s Got Money Troubles' Explores the Realities of OnlyFans Work
item.description: Apple TV+’s adaptation of Rufi Thorpe’s novel, 'Margo’s Got Money Troubles,' offers a nuanced portrayal of sex work, specifically through the lens of OnlyFans. The series follows Margo Millet, a 20-year-old college dropout and single mother, who turns to OnlyFans after losing her job and facing financial pressures. Unlike sensationalized depictions of sex work, the show emphasizes the creative and human aspects of the profession, showing Margo building a following, developing a persona called Hungry Ghost, and collaborating with other creators. The narrative explores how authenticity and humor can drive success on platforms like OnlyFans, highlighting the daily realities and challenges of digital sex work rather than exaggerating extremes. The show contrasts sharply with HBO’s 'Euphoria,' which portrays online sex work in darker, more shocking ways, focusing on humiliation and controversy. 'Margo’s Got Money Troubles' aims to normalize the profession and depict it as a legitimate form

item.title: The History and Legacy of the Hardy Tree at St Pancras Old Church
item.description: The Hardy Tree was a famous ash tree in the churchyard of St Pancras Old Church, London, known for the dense cluster of Victorian gravestones encircling its base. Traditionally, the arrangement of the headstones has been attributed to the young Thomas Hardy, who supervised the respectful exhumation and relocation of graves during the 1860s construction of the Midland Railway line. While the story has become a cherished literary legend, historical research suggests that the gravestones were likely placed later as a rockery, and the tree grew naturally within the pile during the mid-20th century. Over the years, the Hardy Tree became a symbol of the passage of time, nature reclaiming human spaces, and the blending of life and death in a historical context. By 2014, the tree had been weakened by a parasitic fungus, and following winter storms, it collapsed on December 27, 2022. Fortunately, the surrounding gravestones were mostly unharmed. In 2024, a replacement tree was planted to honor

item.title: Sherilyn Fenn’s Iconic Role as Audrey Horne in Twin Peaks and Her Impact on 1990s Television
item.description: Sherilyn Fenn’s portrayal of Audrey Horne in the original Twin Peaks (1990–1991) remains one of the most memorable performances in television history. Audrey, the neglected daughter of local tycoon Ben Horne, evolves from a rebellious, spoiled teenager into a character concerned with the town’s moral decay. Her unrequited crush on Special Agent Dale Cooper and daring adventures, such as going undercover at One-Eyed Jacks, illustrate both her bravery and naivety. Fenn’s performance combined classic film noir ‘femme fatale’ traits with vulnerability and charm, bringing depth to Audrey beyond a mere troublemaker. Visual elements, including her saddle shoes, pleated skirts, and cashmere sweaters, established a distinctive “retro-modern” look that influenced 1990s fashion. Iconic moments like tying a cherry stem with her tongue and dancing spontaneously to Angelo Badalamenti’s 'Audrey’s Dance' in the Double R Diner cemented her cultural impact. Fenn infused Audrey with a soulful melancholy,

item.title: Clark Gable's Career and Life in the 1950s
item.description: Clark Gable, often hailed as the 'King of Hollywood,' experienced a significant evolution in his career during the 1950s. Having appeared in over 60 films across a range of genres, Gable transitioned from his classic romantic leading roles of the 1930s to more mature, rugged characters in his fifties. After years as MGM's star, he left the studio in 1954 to work as a freelancer, which allowed him greater creative freedom and financial control. Notable successes during this period included 'Lone Star' (1952) and 'Mogambo' (1953), the latter showcasing his enduring on-screen chemistry with younger actresses such as Grace Kelly and Ava Gardner. The 1950s saw Gable embrace roles in Westerns, like 'The Tall Men' (1955) and 'The King and Four Queens' (1956), highlighting his outdoorsman persona. One of his most acclaimed late-career performances came in 'Run Silent, Run Deep' (1958), where he portrayed a disciplined submarine commander, proving he could carry a film without relying on romant

item.title: Immigrants at Desert View Annex Stage Hunger Strike Over Detention Conditions
item.description: At least 20 immigrants detained at the Desert View Annex in Adelanto, California, have begun a hunger strike to protest substandard living conditions. The strike, organized by a coalition of immigrant rights groups including the Defend Migrants Alliance of Southern California, calls attention to mold, unsafe drinking water, delayed medical care, and reduced food portions that force detainees to buy costly commissary items. The annex, which holds over 400 detainees despite a 750-bed capacity, is adjacent to the Adelanto ICE Processing Center, where four detainees have died since its reopening. Family members and attorneys have reported that medical requests are often ignored or minimally addressed, with detainees receiving basic over-the-counter remedies instead of proper care. Geo Group Inc., the private company managing the facility, maintains that its operations comply with federal detention standards monitored by ICE and the Department of Homeland Security. This strike highlights on

item.title: Democratic Lawmakers Threaten Repeated Expulsion Votes Over Candidate's Antisemitic Remarks
item.description: Two Democratic lawmakers, Reps. Jared Moskowitz of Florida and Josh Gottheimer of New Jersey, have pledged to repeatedly vote to expel Texas Democratic candidate Maureen Galindo from Congress if she wins the upcoming midterm election. Galindo, a sex therapist running in a Republican-leaning district, has made controversial statements calling for the imprisonment of 'American Zionists and former ICE officers' and broadly claiming that Zionists are involved in pedophilia. Moskowitz emphasized his commitment, stating he would vote to expel her every single day if necessary. Galindo won the most votes in the Democratic primary and will face a runoff election. Democratic leaders, including House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, condemned her remarks as disqualifying. Both Moskowitz and Gottheimer highlighted a broader rise in antisemitism in politics and urged both parties to have zero tolerance for antisemitic rhetoric. Some prominent Democrats, however, have associated with controversial

item.title: Despite Low Confidence, American Consumers Keep Spending
item.description: American consumers continue to spend aggressively despite widespread dissatisfaction with the economy. Retailers like Walmart, Target, Home Depot, and Lowe’s reported strong earnings this week, surprising analysts with both their current performance and optimistic outlooks for 2026. Even companies previously struggling, such as Gap, Starbucks, and Chipotle, are seeing a resurgence as customers return. Analysts attribute this phenomenon to factors including higher tax refunds, wealthier Americans benefiting from stock market gains, and a cultural tendency to maintain spending habits despite inflation pressures. Core consumer spending grew by 0.5% in April, signaling ongoing resilience even as gas prices rise and overall consumer sentiment remains at historic lows. However, risks remain. Prolonged high gas prices, fading tax refund benefits, and broader economic uncertainty could eventually reduce spending. Wealthier households, who experienced income growth exceeding inflation, are driv

item.title: Trump's Approval Rating Hits Near Record Lows in Latest Fox News Poll
item.description: A recent Fox News poll has revealed a significant drop in President Donald Trump's approval ratings, alarming both the public and media commentators. On MSNBC's 'Morning Joe,' co-host Mika Brzezinski highlighted the figures, noting that Trump's overall job performance stands at just 39 percent. This represents a three-point decrease from last month and a ten-point drop since the beginning of his second term. Only one point separates this rating from his lowest ever, recorded in October 2017 during his first term. Meanwhile, his disapproval rate has reached a record high of 61 percent. Voter dissatisfaction is especially pronounced regarding the economy, with 71 percent expressing disapproval of Trump's economic handling, marking a five-point increase from last month and a 15-point rise compared to a year ago. Interestingly, this includes a notable seven-point jump in disapproval among Republican voters. On foreign policy, two-thirds of respondents believe the United States is winning t

item.title: Trump and Vance Push Railroad Provisions in Highway Bill Amid GOP Division
item.description: President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance are advocating for amendments to the five-year highway reauthorization bill that would include the Rail Safety Act, mandating two-person freight train crews—a long-sought goal for unions. The proposed amendment, however, is creating tension within the House Republican caucus. Conservative members and the railroad industry oppose it, citing concerns about labor concessions. House Majority Leader Steve Scalise and Speaker Mike Johnson acknowledged the controversy, noting that it complicates the bill's passage. Despite opposition, some lawmakers, like Rep. David Rouzer, expect the amendment to pass, which would represent a significant labor win and potentially draw Democratic support despite other disagreements. The effort reflects Vance's ongoing focus on railroad safety and labor issues, particularly following the East Palestine derailment, which exposed unsafe railroad practices. The debate highlights the challenge of balancing White H

item.title: Where the Cast of Coyote Ugly Are Today, 26 Years Later
item.description: Coyote Ugly, released over 25 years ago, captured audiences with its vibrant depiction of a New York bar and a cast full of emerging stars. The film followed Violet Sanford, played by Piper Perabo, a young singer who moves to New York to pursue her dreams and lands a job at the Coyote Ugly bar. Since then, the cast has taken diverse career paths. Piper Perabo transitioned from film to television, earning a Golden Globe nomination for Covert Affairs and later joining Yellowstone and The Hunting Party. Supermodel-turned-actress Tyra Banks, who played bartender Zoe, became the face of America's Next Top Model and later hosted Dancing with the Stars. Maria Bello, who portrayed bar owner Lil, continued acting in TV and film, including NCIS and Goliath. Izabella Miko, Cammie in the film, pursued acting and appeared in Save the Last Dance 2, Step Up: All In, and NCIS: Hawai'i. Bridget Moynahan went on to a successful TV career, appearing in Blue Bloods and Six Degrees. John Goodman, already e

item.title: Trump Administration's IRS Settlement and Its Implications for Accountability
item.description: The article reports on a highly controversial IRS settlement orchestrated during the Trump administration, describing it as a $1.776 billion fund allegedly intended to reward individuals who claim to have been harmed by the Biden Administration. The settlement, arranged by Acting Attorney General Todd Blanch, allows Trump to appoint and remove commission members without oversight, and limits public and congressional review of the fund's recipients. Critics argue this effectively rewards violent or illegal actions by loyalists and pardoned January 6 participants, framing the government as prioritizing loyalty over rule of law. The article claims that Trump and his allies have created a system where misconduct is financially compensated, raising serious ethical and legal concerns. Additionally, a DOJ order reportedly bars future tax or legal actions against Trump and his entities, potentially granting him immunity from prosecution. The piece highlights fears of systemic corruption, erosi

item.title: Intimate 1993 Polaroids of Kate Moss Captured by Ellen von Unwerth for Vogue
item.description: In July 1993, iconic supermodel Kate Moss was photographed by Ellen von Unwerth during a Vogue US shoot titled 'Beauty and the Beach' in Miami. These images were captured on type 100 peel-apart Polaroid film, a medium commonly used by photographers to preview how a full photoshoot might unfold. Von Unwerth described one particular Polaroid as a spontaneous moment over coffee, emphasizing the intimate quality and closeness the format provides. Years later, these Polaroids were showcased in the 2022 'Ultimate Ellen von Unwerth' exhibitions held in London and Paris. The collection garnered attention at auction, with individual images fetching prices ranging from £5,000 to £44,000, reflecting both the model's iconic status and the photographer's legendary reputation. The photographs not only highlight Kate Moss’s early career in the 1990s fashion scene but also provide insight into von Unwerth's distinctive style of capturing candid, personal moments that transcend conventional fashion pho

item.title: Google Introduces AI-Powered Search Mode with Interactive Features
item.description: Google has rolled out a major update to its search engine, introducing an 'intelligent search box' powered by its Gemini 3.5 Flash AI model. This new 'AI Mode' allows users to engage in a continuous conversational search experience, including the ability to upload photos and videos for analysis. For example, the AI correctly identified a vacation photo as being from Torres del Paine National Park in Chile. AI Mode displays detailed, bulleted answers with citations and enables follow-up questions within the same interface. Traditional blue-link search results remain available, and users can opt out of AI Mode at any time. Google also plans to enhance personalized notifications through AI agents for monitoring topics of interest, like updates from favorite musicians, initially available to AI Pro and Ultra subscribers. While the feature aims to streamline search and keep users within Google, caution is advised as AI-generated answers can be inaccurate, sometimes offering absurd suggestio

item.title: Former Border Patrol Chief Criticizes Tom Homan Over Immigration Policy
item.description: Tom Homan, President Donald Trump's top immigration adviser, is facing criticism from outgoing Border Patrol commander Greg Bovino. Bovino, recently forced into retirement, claims that Homan's approach to immigration enforcement is insufficiently aggressive, arguing that Homan only targets immigrants with known criminal records. Bovino exaggerates the number of unauthorized immigrants in the U.S., claiming '100 million' are evading enforcement, while official estimates range between 11 and 14 million. Homan, who succeeded Bovino, has a controversial reputation, including an incident involving accepting money from undercover FBI agents. Despite his hardline rhetoric, actual deportation numbers under the Trump administration have fallen short of the stated goal of one million removals annually, with approximately 460,000 deportations per year according to UC Berkeley data. This public feud highlights broader questions about the effectiveness and priorities of U.S. immigration policy unde

item.title: Todd Blanche Defends Trump in DOJ Matters, Drawing Criticism for Potential Conflicts
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche has recently taken a highly visible role in defending former President Donald Trump, both in public interviews and before Senate committees. Unlike his predecessor, Pam Bondi, Blanche has been described as more willing to advocate for Trump's initiatives, including matters involving the Justice Department's so-called 'weaponization' fund. Analysts note that Blanche's approach is professional and calculated; he answers questions from the press and appears to strategically advance Trump's interests. Critics have raised concerns about the independence of settlements overseen by commissioners Blanche appoints, who could potentially be removed at Trump's discretion. While Congress could intervene, Blanche's actions highlight a continued tension over the Justice Department's role in political matters and whether legal processes are being used to benefit allies of the former president. This coverage has sparked debate over the ethical and legal implication

item.title: Trump Surgeon General Nominee Criticized for Using Farmland Tax Loophole Despite Not Farming
item.description: Donald Trump's nominee for Surgeon General, Nicole Saphier, a Fox News contributor and radiologist, is under scrutiny for claiming a New Jersey farmland tax break despite not engaging in actual farming. Saphier owns a 16,000-square-foot estate in Far Hills, NJ, purchased for $5.8 million in 2023, and has designated 10.35 acres as farmland, reducing her property taxes on that land to about $40 annually. This designation significantly lowers her property value for tax purposes by approximately $624,500, compared to the standard $7,200 she would otherwise owe. Saphier herself acknowledged in an Instagram post that she is 'not a real farmer' but 'pretends like she is,' which conflicts with her formal property tax application. Officials overseeing the farmland program have expressed concerns about the misuse of the tax incentive, which was intended to preserve agricultural land rather than subsidize luxury estates. Critics argue that such practices undermine the integrity of the program, wh

item.title: Column Criticizes Trump for Controversial $1.776 Billion Fund Linked to IRS Settlement
item.description: A recent Guardian column by Moira Donegan sharply criticizes President Donald Trump's handling of a $1.776 billion fund, calling it an 'extraordinary incident of bald self-dealing.' The fund, labeled as an 'anti-weaponization fund,' was created following Trump's settlement of a $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS over leaked tax returns. According to Donegan, the structure of the fund ensures that Trump maintains near-total control, with four out of five commissioners appointed by his own attorney general and Trump retaining the power to dismiss them at will. The settlement also obligates the IRS to drop all audits of Trump and his family. The timing of the agreement, just before a federal judge's May 20 deadline, drew particular scorn for seemingly bypassing legal scrutiny. Donegan warns that such actions could leave a lasting legacy of corruption, setting precedents for future administrations and fostering voter cynicism about government integrity. The column suggests that many Ameri

item.title: Investigation Underway After Southern California Couple Found Dead; Possible Romance Scam Involved
item.description: Karen and Donald Whitaker, a couple in their late 70s, were found dead last week in their Bermuda Dunes home in Coachella Valley. Riverside County sheriff’s deputies discovered the couple after a neighbor requested a wellness check. Authorities reported that both Karen, 79, and Donald, 80, had suffered traumatic injuries and were declared dead at the scene. Prior to their deaths, Karen Whitaker had reportedly been communicating with a person claiming to be actor Tom Selleck. According to family friends, the impersonator reached out to Karen after she posted a memorial on Facebook, convincing her to send money through gift cards in increasing amounts. Friends and family attempted to intervene by removing access to her bank accounts and credit cards, and even someone with connections to Tom Selleck confirmed that the actor was not involved. While authorities are investigating the circumstances of the deaths, it is currently unclear whether the alleged scam had any direct connection to th

item.title: U.S. Intelligence Reports Show Iran Recovering Faster Than Expected After Strikes
item.description: U.S. intelligence assessments indicate that Iran's military recovery is progressing faster than what U.S. and Israeli officials initially claimed. CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper testified that Operation Epic Fury had destroyed 90% of Iran's defense industrial base and prevented Iran from reconstituting its military for years. However, multiple sources familiar with intelligence assessments told CNN that Iran has already restarted some drone production, with full drone attack capabilities potentially restored within six months. Despite U.S. and Israeli strikes, roughly two-thirds of Iran's missile launchers survived, partly due to the recent ceasefire allowing Iran to recover hidden launchers. While the strikes caused some delays, the damage is considered a setback measured in months, not years. Intelligence also suggests that China continued supplying missile components, though a U.S. naval blockade slowed this flow. This discrepancy between official testimony and intelligence a

item.title: Overcoming Payment Barriers for Digital Products in Restricted Countries
item.description: The article describes the challenges of selling digital products in countries where mainstream payment platforms like PayPal and Stripe consider operations 'high-risk.' Initially, the business struggled to onboard international customers due to these platform restrictions. Alternative payment methods such as bank transfers and cryptocurrency were explored but led to poor customer experience and declining sales. Setting up a separate legal entity abroad was also considered but dismissed due to high legal and operational costs. Eventually, the team implemented a custom payment gateway in partnership with a regional processor, offering local payment methods familiar to their customers. This approach reduced payment processing fees by 40%, improved conversion rates by 25%, and helped recover sales while providing a competitive edge in the regional e-learning market. The author reflects that investing in a custom gateway earlier could have accelerated growth, emphasizing that platform restr

item.title: Former Trump Envoy Advises Caution in Iran Negotiations Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Former White House spokeswoman and Iran negotiator Morgan Ortagus warned that Iran may be using ongoing nuclear negotiations to delay U.S. pressure while preserving leverage. Speaking at the Middle East Forum in Washington, Ortagus emphasized that Tehran historically draws out diplomatic talks as a strategy to buy time. She cautioned President Trump not to fall into this pattern while pursuing diplomacy and temporarily pausing planned military strikes. Ortagus highlighted that the U.S. currently has more negotiating leverage than past administrations, pointing to Trump’s firm stance on Iran’s nuclear program and regional proxies. She also discussed the Israel-Lebanon front, explaining that Hezbollah, acting independently of the Lebanese government, continues to obstruct peace efforts, complicating ceasefire enforcement. Ortagus stressed that both Lebanon and Israel share interests in stability but Hezbollah’s influence must be addressed to achieve progress. She described the current co

item.title: Trump Administration Eases FDA Rules on Flavored Vapes Following Reynolds American Donation to Super PAC
item.description: A New York Times report has sparked controversy after revealing that Reynolds American, the maker of Vuse vapes, donated $5 million to President Donald Trump's super PAC, MAGA Inc., on April 30, 2026. This brought the company's total contributions to the group to $8 million. Just days later, Reynolds executives and lobbyists, along with representatives from Altria, met with Trump at his Jupiter, Florida golf club. During the lunch, they voiced complaints about FDA regulations on e-cigarettes. Trump reportedly interrupted the meeting to call FDA Commissioner Marty Makary and, when unsuccessful, contacted Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to express dissatisfaction with vape policies. Less than a week after the donation, the FDA issued new guidance that could enable major tobacco companies to market flavored vapes more easily, potentially allowing them to capture a larger share of the $6 billion e-cigarette market by deprioritizing enforcement against certain unauthorized products.

item.title: A Comprehensive Guide to Comfortable Shoes Recommended by Reviewers
item.description: This article highlights 30 different pairs of shoes that are highly praised for their comfort by customers and reviewers alike. It features a mix of sandals, sneakers, loafers, ballet flats, and wedges, including vegan and memory foam options. Each shoe is described with its key comfort features, such as cushioned insoles, arch support, lightweight materials, and breathable designs. Many of the shoes are suitable for various activities, from casual walking and running errands to attending events or dancing for hours. The list includes budget-friendly options as well as higher-end designer alternatives. Brands mentioned range from JW Pei and Steve Madden to Allbirds, Rothy's, and Dr. Martens, showcasing both small businesses and established brands. Each shoe comes with a recommended purchase link and price range, along with a real customer review highlighting the comfort and style. Overall, the article serves as a detailed guide for anyone looking to combine fashion with all-day wearabi

item.title: A Look at The Elks Magazine Covers from the 1920s
item.description: The Elks Magazine, established in 1922, has served as the official publication for the Benevolent and Protective Order of Elks, connecting the national organization with its members across the United States. During the 1920s, the magazine gained a reputation for its high-quality production and artistic covers created by notable illustrators of the time. Its content ranged from fraternal news to short stories by prominent American authors, reflecting both cultural and social trends of early 20th-century America. Today, the magazine continues to document the Elks' charitable efforts, including programs for veterans and youth, while preserving a historical record of civic engagement and community spirit. The curated collection featured in this article highlights thirty vintage covers from the 1920s, showcasing the evolving art and design styles of the era and offering valuable insight for historians and enthusiasts of American fraternal culture.

item.title: Effective Strategies for Supporting Employees During Medical Leave
item.description: Serious health issues, whether a car accident, cancer, or mental health crisis, can turn daily life upside down, making work seem overwhelming. For employees, medical leave can be a crucial lifeline, but many delay taking time off due to fear of job loss, perceived lack of commitment, or financial concerns. According to research published in the Journal of Applied Psychology in March 2026, employers can design medical leave policies that actively support recovery rather than add stress. Interviews with 30 employees and 18 HR professionals revealed that access to paid leave is essential but not enough. Best practices include training HR specialists and managers to clearly communicate benefits, ensuring cross-training of coworkers to cover tasks, and making leave policies available from the first day of employment. Employees should check their eligibility under the Family and Medical Leave Act and explore short-term disability or state-paid leave programs. Importantly, employees can main

item.title: A Look at Humorous and Medical-Themed Valentine’s Cards from the Early 1900s
item.description: In the early 20th century, Valentine’s Day cards often combined humor with medical themes, reflecting the era’s fascination with patent medicines and health remedies. Companies like Smith’s Bile Beans and Lash’s Bitters produced cards featuring colorful, amusing illustrations alongside promotional messages for their products. A common motif showed a man suffering from an ailment, humorously suggesting that curing his 'sluggish bile' would win a woman's affection. Beyond lighthearted health advice, some cards—known as 'Vinegar Valentines'—were intentionally insulting and sent anonymously to people considered unpleasant, often exaggerating physical or personality traits. Over time, the depiction of nurses evolved from stern, sour-faced figures to heroic and romanticized characters, particularly after World War I. By the 1920s and 1930s, nurses were frequently portrayed mending broken hearts, literally sewing love back together in playful imagery. These cards were printed using chromolith

item.title: Catherine Deneuve: A Legendary Career in French and Global Cinema
item.description: Catherine Deneuve is celebrated as one of the most enduring icons of French cinema, known for her elegance, sophistication, and versatility on screen. She first gained international recognition in the 1960s with her role in Jacques Demy’s musical 'The Umbrellas of Cherbourg' (1964), showcasing her charm and musical talent. Deneuve then delivered a haunting performance in Roman Polanski’s psychological thriller 'Repulsion' (1965), solidifying her range as an actress. She became a muse for renowned director Luis Buñuel, most notably in 'Belle de Jour' (1967), where she portrayed a woman balancing a seemingly reserved exterior with a complex and secretive inner life. Beyond these iconic roles, Deneuve demonstrated remarkable adaptability, earning critical acclaim for performances in films like 'The Last Metro' and 'Indochine', the latter of which earned her an Academy Award nomination. Her influence extends beyond film; she once embodied Marianne, the national symbol of the French Republi

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel may qualify for compensation from Trump-linked fund
item.description: The New York Times reports that FBI Director Kash Patel could potentially receive millions from a controversial $1.8 billion fund reportedly linked to former President Donald Trump. The fund, originally associated with compensating allies involved in the January 6 insurrection, is now reportedly open to a broader group of administration officials due to vague eligibility criteria. Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche informed a Senate committee that there are 'no limits' on applicants, which could include Patel. Patel has previously claimed to be a target of government 'weaponization,' and his testimony before Congress emphasizes his view of being unfairly targeted by the FBI. The fund’s commissioners, yet to be appointed, will determine eligibility based on their interpretation of 'weaponization.' If an expansive interpretation is adopted, it could also benefit other public figures, including media personalities like Jim Hoft. Trump's statements and actions suggest that the fund may c

item.title: WHO Raises Alarm Over Rapid Spread of Rare Ebola Strain in Eastern Congo
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has raised serious concerns over a rapidly spreading outbreak of a rare Ebola strain, Bundibugyo, in eastern Congo. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and over 500 suspected cases, with the first death occurring on April 24. The outbreak initially went undetected because early tests targeted the more common Zaire Ebola type, resulting in delayed response and worsening the situation. WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus highlighted the emergence of cases in urban areas, deaths of healthcare workers, and significant population movement as major concerns. Neighboring Uganda has reported two confirmed cases, including one death in its capital, Kampala. The Bundibugyo strain has no approved vaccines or treatments, though experimental vaccines for other Ebola types are expected to arrive from the U.S. and U.K. Humanitarian aid is being deployed, including relief supplies from UNICEF, and support from organizations like Docto

item.title: New Zealand Warriors Secure Key Halves with Re-signings
item.description: The New Zealand Warriors are solidifying their halves lineup for the upcoming seasons with the confirmation of one re-signing and progress on another. Following Luke Metcalf's planned departure to the St George Illawarra Dragons at the end of the year, the club will rely on Chanel Harris-Tavita, Te Maire Martin, Jett Cleary, and Luke Hanson to lead the team. Harris-Tavita, who had attracted interest from the North Queensland Cowboys, is confirmed to remain with the Warriors, according to CEO Cameron George. Meanwhile, discussions are ongoing with Te Maire Martin regarding his future with the club, with positive news expected soon. Coach Andrew Webster emphasized the importance of retaining these key players to maintain the team's competitive form. With Luke Hanson linked to the Gold Coast Titans and other emerging talents like Jett Cleary approaching first-grade debut, the Warriors are strategically planning for stability and growth in their halves combination. Current squad member Jye

item.title: Robert De Niro Delivers Sharp Critique of Trump During Stephen Colbert’s Last 'Colbert Questionert' Segment
item.description: During the second-to-last episode of “The Late Show” on May 21, 2026, host Stephen Colbert’s usual celebrity segment, the “Colbert Questionert,” took an unexpected turn when Oscar-winning actor Robert De Niro appeared as a guest. Traditionally, Colbert asks his guests playful or personal questions, but De Niro, known for his outspoken criticism of former President Donald Trump, flipped the routine. When Colbert asked a question about a number he was thinking of, De Niro delivered a sharp political jab, referencing the still-unreleased Epstein files linked to Trump. The audience reacted with laughter and applause, and Colbert himself was visibly amused. The moment became one of the highlights of the episode, demonstrating how celebrities continue to use late-night platforms for political commentary.
