However, after reviewing her blood work results, which clearly showed the negative impact of halting them, she decided to resume the full regimen. Kardashian emphasized the importance of the supplements for her overall health and wellness, noting that the blood tests provided convincing evidence to continue. In a lighthearted moment, she joked about wishing for a simpler so

item.title: Stephen Colbert Reveals Unused Graphics as 'Late Show' Nears End
item.description: Stephen Colbert began the final week of his nearly 11-year tenure on 'The Late Show' by showcasing previously unseen graphics and sketches that never aired. In a segment titled 'Graphics Graveyard,' named after a Slack channel where the show's staff discarded unused ideas, Colbert revealed a graphic declaring Hillary Clinton the winner of the 2016 presidential election—an image that ultimately did not make it to broadcast. Other unaired graphics included a satirical Thanksgiving magazine called Giblets and a pun-filled Martha Stewart Living cover reading 'Donner party or dinner party.' Colbert's farewell week will feature high-profile guests such as Jon Stewart, Steven Spielberg, David Byrne, and Bruce Springsteen. The finale’s programming remains partially undisclosed. CBS’s decision to end the show has been controversial, with critics suggesting political motives linked to Trump amid Paramount Global’s Skydance Media merger, although the network maintains the decision was financial,

item.title: GOP Declines to Fund Trump-Endorsed Texas Candidate Amid Controversy
item.description: Following his recent primary victories, former President Donald Trump's influence in the Republican Party has faced pushback with his endorsement of Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton. Paxton, who has faced multiple scandals, is now likely to be the Republican nominee for the U.S. Senate seat currently held by John Cornyn. However, GOP leadership has made it clear that they will not allocate party funds for Paxton's general election campaign. Conservative journalist David Drucker reported on MS NOW's “Morning Joe” that the party views Paxton as Trump's responsibility due to Trump's considerable personal political resources, which range between $350 million and $400 million. The party plans to prioritize funding for other races in states like North Carolina and Maine instead. This move highlights the tension within the Republican Party between Trump's influence and the establishment's strategic priorities, as they attempt to balance high-profile endorsements with overall campaign resourc

item.title: Key Races and Political Stakes in Tuesday’s Primaries Across Six States
item.description: On May 19, 2026, six states—Alabama, Georgia, Idaho, Kentucky, Oregon, and Pennsylvania—hold primaries, making it one of the busiest days in the election calendar. The spotlight is on Kentucky, where President Donald Trump is targeting Rep. Thomas Massie in a highly expensive and symbolic House primary, reflecting Trump’s ongoing effort to challenge GOP members who have opposed him. Meanwhile, in Georgia, Governor Brian Kemp’s endorsement of political newcomer Derek Dooley for Senate will test Kemp’s influence against Trump-aligned candidates, likely leading to a June 16 runoff. Other notable contests include Kentucky Rep. Andy Barr aiming for Mitch McConnell’s Senate seat, Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones in a tight gubernatorial primary, and Alabama’s Rep. Barry Moore facing a competitive Senate race. Democrats are also aiming to flip two state Supreme Court seats in Georgia, signaling potential trends for future elections. Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro’s reelection campaign seeks to

item.title: Google Launches Gemini Omni AI for Conversational Video and Media Editing
item.description: At Google I/O 2026, Google unveiled Gemini Omni, a new multimodal artificial intelligence system designed to simplify creative workflows across video, images, audio, and text. Gemini Omni allows users to edit and generate media using natural language, either through voice or text commands, eliminating the need for traditional complex editing software. Demonstrations at the conference showcased users transforming ordinary video scenes into cinematic experiences, altering backgrounds, generating sound effects, and refining projects with simple conversational prompts. Google emphasized that the system is 'omni-modal,' meaning it can handle multiple media types within a single workflow. The first version, Gemini Omni Flash, is expected to roll out around September 2026, although official pricing has not yet been announced. Some advanced creative features may be limited to premium or enterprise subscriptions. This launch highlights Google’s ongoing push to integrate AI more deeply into its

item.title: Behind the Scenes with Brigitte Bardot in 'Babette Goes to War' (1959)
item.description: The portraits of Brigitte Bardot taken during the filming of 'Babette Goes to War' in 1959 provide a unique glimpse into the French actress at a pivotal moment in her career. Known primarily for her image as a 1950s sex symbol, Bardot is shown in military-inspired costumes while maintaining her signature beehive hairstyle, a look that would go on to influence global fashion trends. The photographs capture both candid moments of laughter and carefully posed promotional shots, revealing a playful and rebellious side of Bardot that challenged conventional ideas of femininity during that era. This collection not only documents the height of 'Bardot-mania' but also reflects the cinematic style and culture of the late 1950s, highlighting the evolution of celebrity imagery and the era’s approach to glamour and publicity. These portraits act as a cultural time capsule, celebrating both Bardot’s star power and the broader fashion and film trends of the decade.

item.title: Jefferson and Madison’s Views on Religious Freedom and Government
item.description: On May 17, 2026, thousands of Americans gathered at the National Mall for 'Rededicate 250,' a prayer rally aimed at honoring the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary. Led by U.S. House Speaker Mike Johnson and other conservative figures, the event sought to 'rededicate the United States of America as one nation under God.' While the rally drew political and religious attention, it also sparked criticism for blurring the lines between church and state. Historical analysis shows that Founding Fathers Thomas Jefferson and James Madison championed religious freedom and strict separation of church and state. Jefferson authored the Virginia Bill for Religious Freedom, ensuring liberty of conscience and protection from government interference in religious affairs. Madison defended similar principles in his 'Memorial and Remonstrance Against Religious Assessments,' opposing government funding for religious instruction. Both men were committed to ensuring broad religious liberties as es

item.title: Trump's Primary Endorsements and Policy Priorities Spark Internal GOP Concerns Ahead of 2026 Midterms
item.description: According to a NOTUS report, President Donald Trump is prioritizing personal and loyalist-focused decisions that some Republicans fear could jeopardize the party's narrow majorities in the House and Senate during the 2026 midterm elections. With less than six months until voting, Trump has endorsed controversial figures such as Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton over longtime Sen. John Cornyn, despite significant party resources already invested in Cornyn's reelection. This move has strained relations within the Senate GOP, particularly after Cornyn's history of supporting Trump, including his role as whip during the first Trump administration. Republicans worry the primary fight will drain funds needed for competitive races in battleground states. Additionally, Trump continues to push for a $1 billion White House ballroom renovation, which has complicated funding discussions for Secret Service operations and drawn criticism amid public concerns over inflation and rising costs. The admi

item.title: Lahore Restores Historical Names and Heritage Sites to Revive Cultural Legacy
item.description: Lahore, one of Pakistan's major cities, is undergoing a significant cultural restoration project nearly 80 years after the partition. The Lahore Heritage Area Revival (LHAR) initiative, launched in 2025 under Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz and initially envisioned by former Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif, aims to restore historical Hindu, Sikh, Jain, and colonial-era names to the city's streets and neighborhoods. Key changes include reverting Islampura to Krishan Nagar, Rehman Gali to Ram Gali, and Sunnat Nagar to Sant Nagar, among others. Colonial names like Lawrence Gardens and Queens Road have also been reinstated. Beyond street renaming, the project includes restoring heritage sites such as Minto Park (Greater Iqbal Park), historical cricket grounds, and a traditional wrestling arena known for legends like Gama Pehalwan. This initiative seeks to boost heritage tourism, improve Pakistan’s global image, and attract diplomatic and economic support while signaling social stability to interna

item.title: Geraldine Fitzgerald: Celebrating the Legacy of a Pioneering Irish Actress
item.description: Geraldine Fitzgerald was a celebrated Irish actress whose talent and emotional depth left an indelible mark on Hollywood's Golden Age. Best known for her unforgettable portrayal of Isabella Linton in the 1939 classic 'Wuthering Heights,' Fitzgerald earned an Academy Award nomination for Best Supporting Actress. Unlike many of her peers, she prioritized artistic integrity over studio pressures, which set her apart as an independent and respected figure in the entertainment industry. Beyond film, she had a distinguished Broadway career and later became a pioneer in street theater, demonstrating her enduring commitment to storytelling in its many forms. This article also showcases portraits of Fitzgerald from the late 1930s and 1940s, capturing the elegance and charm of her early career. Her dedication to her craft and her influence on stage and screen continue to inspire actors and audiences alike.

item.title: Gene Laurents: Iconic 1960s Fashion Photography for Vogue and Harper’s Bazaar
item.description: Gene Laurents was an American photographer celebrated for his elegant and cinematic style in mid-20th century fashion photography. Active primarily in the 1950s and 1960s, he contributed extensively to leading publications such as Vogue and Harper’s Bazaar. His images masterfully combined high-fashion sophistication with intimate, human portrayals, often elevating models and celebrities to the level of classical art through soft lighting, precise composition, and thoughtful storytelling. Laurents captured the glamour of the era while reflecting shifting social tides. The article showcases numerous examples of his work, featuring top models like Wilhelmina, Benedetta Barzini, Simone d'Aillencourt, and Brigitte Bardot. Many photos incorporate period automobiles, such as Ford Mustangs, Cadillacs, and the Avanti, blending fashion with automotive design. Standout images include Wilhelmina in Halston and Originala designs, Benedetta Barzini in luxurious furs and modern ensembles by designers

item.title: MS NOW Hosts React with Laughter to Failed Trump Plan to Reinstate Ahmadinejad
item.description: On May 20, 2026, MS NOW hosts Joe Scarborough and John Heilemann reacted with amusement to a report about a failed plan by former President Donald Trump and Israeli officials to reinstall Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as Iran's leader. According to a New York Times report, the plan, which involved consulting Ahmadinejad, quickly fell apart when he was injured on the first day of the conflict by an Israeli strike. This left Ahmadinejad disillusioned with the initiative. During a segment on 'Morning Joe,' the Times' Elizabeth Buhmiller described Trump's position on Iran as problematic and highlighted how the aborted plan revealed a lack of preparation. Scarborough sarcastically commented on the absurdity of the situation, while Heilemann compared the scenario to a 'South Park' episode, underlining the bizarre and unplanned nature of the operation. The segment included humorous references to movies and dramatizations of U.S. intervention, underscoring how the plan's failure became a source of publi

item.title: European Stock Markets Slightly Decline Amid Ongoing Middle East Tensions
item.description: European stock markets experienced a slight decline on May 20, 2026, as ongoing tensions in the Middle East continued to create uncertainty among investors. In London, the FTSE index fell by 0.41%, coinciding with the release of UK inflation data, which showed a decrease to 2.8% in April. In Germany, the DAX index dropped by 0.35%, while producer prices in the country increased by 1.7% year-over-year in April, indicating some inflationary pressures. France's CAC index saw a minor decline of 0.20%. Elsewhere in Europe, Austria reported an annual inflation rate increase to 3.4% in April, signaling that inflationary concerns remain in the region. The market movements reflect cautious investor sentiment as geopolitical risks in the Middle East continue to influence global financial markets. Economic indicators across Europe show a mixed picture, with some easing inflation in certain countries while others report rising prices, contributing to a cautious trading environment. Overall, Europe

item.title: 1954 Jaguar XK120 SE Drophead Coupe: Features and History of the Special Equipment Model
item.description: The 1954 Jaguar XK120 SE Drophead Coupe represents a pinnacle of mid-20th century British automotive design and engineering. As a Special Equipment variant of the iconic XK120 series, this model was enhanced for improved performance and comfort. It featured a powerful 3.4-liter inline-six engine capable of producing 180 brake horsepower, paired with dual exhaust systems and a stiffer suspension setup that delivered a more engaging driving experience compared to standard models. Unlike the minimalist Roadster version, the Drophead Coupe provided greater refinement with its elegant interior appointments, including a polished walnut dashboard, and a robust, lined canvas roof that offered superior protection from the elements while maintaining the open-top appeal of a convertible. Originally introduced in the late 1940s, the XK120 line gained fame for its record-breaking speeds and sleek styling inspired by pre-war racing successes. The SE models built upon this foundation by offering high

item.title: Kim Novak: Iconic 1950s Portraits and Hollywood Journey
item.description: Kim Novak, born on February 13, 1933, is an American retired actress and painter whose impact on cinema has been recognized with multiple awards, including two Golden Globes, a Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement, an Honorary Golden Bear, and a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame. During the 1950s, Novak emerged as one of Hollywood’s most popular 'it' girls, known for her distinctive lavender-tinted blonde hair and her cool, enigmatic screen presence. Discovered in the film The French Line (1953), she signed with Columbia Pictures, which positioned her as a sophisticated alternative to Marilyn Monroe. Novak was the top box-office star for three consecutive years in the mid-to-late 1950s. Despite her fame, she often felt constrained by the studio system and faced controversy, such as her relationship with Sammy Davis Jr., which reportedly ended under pressure from studio head Harry Cohn. By the end of the decade, she began distancing herself from Hollywood's 'bombshell' image and eventu

item.title: Senator Bill Cassidy Supports Resolution Limiting Presidential War Powers After Primary Loss
item.description: Senator Bill Cassidy (R-LA), following his defeat in the Republican primary influenced by President Donald Trump, has taken a notable political stance by voting with Democrats to advance a resolution aimed at limiting the president's authority to initiate military action in Iran. The vote marks a significant moment for Cassidy, signaling a break from party alignment as he becomes a 'free agent' now that his political future in the Senate is ending. Public reactions on social media were mixed, with some commentators acknowledging Cassidy's move as too late to be seen as heroic, while others highlighted his newfound independence from party pressures. Observers noted that this vote aligns him with Democrats on war powers issues, giving them four Republican supporters on the measure. The development has been framed as an example of a politician acting without concern for reelection consequences, with some referring to his actions humorously as joining a 'YOLO caucus.' Cassidy's decision un

item.title: The Cardiff Giant: The Story Behind America’s Famous 19th-Century Hoax
item.description: The Cardiff Giant was a 10-foot-tall gypsum statue that became one of the most notorious archaeological hoaxes in U.S. history. It was “discovered” on October 16, 1869, on a farm in Cardiff, New York, and was presented as the petrified remains of an ancient giant. The hoax was orchestrated by George Hull, a cigar manufacturer, who sought both to mock literal interpretations of the Bible and to profit from public curiosity. Hull had a 5-ton block of gypsum carved in his likeness, treated it to simulate aging, and planted it on William Newell’s farm. When workers excavated it a year later, news spread quickly, and Newell began charging visitors, attracting thousands who believed they were seeing a biblical giant. Even P.T. Barnum attempted to buy the giant for $50,000, and when rebuffed, created a replica and claimed it was the real giant, leading to a legal battle that highlighted the absurdity of the situation. Scientists soon identified the figure as a hoax, noting fresh chisel marks

item.title: Using DNA Analysis of Scat to Protect the Critically Endangered Gilbert’s Potoroo
item.description: Researchers in Western Australia are applying advanced DNA analysis techniques to support conservation efforts for Gilbert’s potoroo, one of the world’s rarest marsupials, with fewer than 150 individuals remaining in the wild. By examining environmental DNA (eDNA) from scat, scientists are able to identify the fungi these marsupials rely on for survival, an essential factor in determining suitable habitats for population recovery. This non-invasive method allows researchers to study the animals' diets without disturbing them and provides valuable insight into how other fungi-eating mammals like quokkas, quendas, and bush rats share overlapping dietary resources. Following the species’ rediscovery in 1994, conservationists have faced challenges in captive breeding due to the potoroo’s selective diet, making wild-to-wild translocations vital. Bushfires in 2015 devastated much of their original habitat, but insurance populations on Bald Island and Waychinicup National Park have safeguarde

item.title: Dr. Amir Khan advises caution with supplements due to risk of overdose and inconsistent dosing
item.description: Dr. Amir Khan has raised concerns about the safety of certain dietary supplements, emphasizing that the industry is not regulated like medicines. Speaking on ITV's Lorraine, he highlighted that many supplements, including popular gummy forms, may contain high levels of sugar, which can lead people to consume excessive amounts. He also pointed out that supplement labels are not always accurate, with some containing much more or less than the recommended dosage. Dr. Khan advises that supplements should only be taken if needed and in forms that encourage consistent use. He recommends three supplements supported by evidence: vitamin D, omega-3 fatty acids, and creatine. Overall, he encourages consulting a GP before starting any new supplement regimen to avoid potential overdose and ensure safe use.

item.title: James Hunt Strikes Track Marshal After Collision at 1977 Canadian Grand Prix
item.description: During the 1977 Canadian Grand Prix at Mosport Park, Formula 1 driver James Hunt became infamous for his aggressive behavior on the track. While battling for the lead against Mario Andretti, Hunt attempted to lap his McLaren teammate Jochen Mass, resulting in a high-speed collision that forced Hunt to retire from the race. Frustrated and enraged, Hunt waved his fists at Mass from the trackside. When track marshal Ernie Strong approached to guide Hunt to safety, Hunt lashed out and struck Strong, knocking him to the ground. Realizing his mistake almost immediately, Hunt helped Strong up and apologized. The race stewards penalized Hunt with a $2,000 fine for assault and an additional $750 for returning to the pit lane unsafely. Strong later pursued legal action over the incident. This episode remains a notable example of Hunt's volatile temperament and is frequently featured in Formula 1 highlights showcasing the sport's most dramatic moments. Hunt later reflected on the incident, attrib

item.title: Reuben Wu Illuminates Landscapes with Drone-Mounted Lights in 'Siren'
item.description: Reuben Wu, a photographer originally from Liverpool and now based in Chicago, creates striking images by using drone-mounted lights to illuminate remote landscapes, a technique he calls 'Aeroglyphs.' His approach transforms light into a dynamic character that interacts with natural environments, producing geometric and fluid forms that appear to dance in the night. Wu's latest project, 'Siren,' captures the interplay of artificial light with natural phenomena, including the rare summer meeting of the Perseid meteor shower and the aurora borealis over Lake Michigan. The project emphasizes a shift from precise geometric patterns to more organic, flowing light forms shaped by the environment itself. Wu stresses the importance of physical presence in his work, contrasting it with AI-generated images, highlighting the unpredictable and immersive experience of creating these photographs in real-time. His influences range from the systematic industrial photography of Bernd and Hilla Becher to

item.title: Sen. John Curtis Signals Concern Over Trump’s Ambassador Picks Kari Lake and Doug Mastriano
item.description: Senator John Curtis (R-UT) has expressed concerns regarding two of former President Donald Trump’s controversial ambassadorial nominees, Kari Lake and Doug Mastriano. Curtis, who serves on the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, told Punchbowl News that his 'radar is up' due to their history of challenging election results. Lake, nominated for ambassador to Jamaica, previously refused to concede her 2022 gubernatorial race in Arizona and denied the 2020 presidential election results. Her leadership of Voice of America involved mass layoffs later deemed unlawful by a federal court. Mastriano, nominated for ambassador to Slovakia, was present outside the Capitol during the January 6, 2021, insurrection and has also sought to overturn the 2020 election. Curtis emphasized that while his initial impressions are critical, he remains open to hearing the nominees' perspectives on the role. Both nominations face additional hurdles, as other Republican senators, including Thom Tillis (R-NC), hav

item.title: Daesung Lee’s 'Nirvana' Explores Resilience and Humor in the Lives of Korean Women Through Portraits with His Mother
item.description: In 'Nirvana,' Korean photographer Daesung Lee collaborates with his mother, Yi-Boun Shin, to explore the hidden lives and resilience of women from her generation. The project was inspired by a profound conversation about reincarnation, where his mother revealed that her life had been too difficult to live again. Lee's photography captures both the hardships and humor that characterize the experiences of older Korean women, reflecting broader historical and cultural contexts, including patriarchal expectations and societal pressures. By working closely with his mother, Lee transforms everyday objects and settings into surreal, playful, yet poignant compositions. Shin, traditionally defined by her roles as wife and mother, becomes an active artistic collaborator, allowing the project to explore identity, memory, and generational dynamics. The photographs highlight the paradoxical coexistence of struggle and humor, a survival mechanism rooted in Korea’s tumultuous history. Beyond document

item.title: Spencer Pratt, Reality TV Star, Announces Run for Los Angeles Mayor Amid Criticism
item.description: Spencer Pratt, best known for his role on MTV's reality show 'The Hills,' is running for mayor of Los Angeles, drawing sharp comparisons to former President Donald Trump. Pratt, who gained notoriety as Heidi Montag's controversial partner, has a history of bankruptcy and questionable business ventures, including a crystal shop. His political platform centers mainly on opposing the current mayor, Karen Bass, and leveraging personal misfortunes, such as losing his home in the Palisades Fire, for political gain. Critics argue Pratt is using a Trump-like strategy, combining theatrical public appearances, unsubstantiated claims, and attempts to profit from his candidacy, including potential reality show deals. While he portrays himself as a champion of the common citizen, his lifestyle and actions suggest otherwise, as he currently resides in a high-end hotel. Public figures, including Chelsea Handler, have openly criticized him, emphasizing his lack of qualifications and experience in gove

item.title: Georgia GOP Shifts Toward Trump-Aligned Candidates in Key Primaries
item.description: Georgia's Republican Party has experienced a significant shift as Trump-aligned candidates dominated Tuesday's primaries, signaling a clear move away from the old guard. High-profile figures who opposed Trump's 2020 election claims, including Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger, Attorney General Chris Carr, and former aide Gabriel Sterling, were defeated. Meanwhile, Trump-backed candidates like Lt. Gov. Burt Jones, billionaire Rick Jackson, and Rep. Mike Collins advanced to runoffs or won outright, demonstrating the growing influence of Trump-style politics within the state GOP. Analysts note that success in Republican primaries now strongly depends on either the president’s endorsement or the candidate’s ability to demonstrate alignment with Trump. This transformation follows the gradual shift that began after the 2022 midterms and mirrors broader trends across the U.S., where Trump-endorsed candidates have unseated long-standing Republicans. While this strategy consolidates support

item.title: A Glimpse Into the Golden Age of Photography Through Vintage Portraits
item.description: This article presents a curated collection of forty vintage photographs capturing people proudly holding their cameras, spanning the 1920s to the 1950s. The images feature a wide array of cameras, from large format box cameras and folding Kodaks to twin-lens reflex and Leica rangefinders, highlighting the evolution of photography equipment during the early to mid-20th century. Beyond showcasing the cameras themselves, these portraits reveal the pride, joy, and passion that individuals felt in an era when photography was an art form and a treasured hobby. Whether amateur photographers, travelers, or enthusiasts showing off their latest acquisitions, the people in these photographs reflect the cultural and technological significance of analog photography before the rise of digital cameras. The article emphasizes the human connection to these tools, illustrating how photography was not merely a hobby but a symbol of creativity and personal expression. Overall, this collection offers reade

item.title: New Computer Science Graduates Enter Job Market Amid Rising AI Challenges
item.description: As computer science students like Audrey Hasson graduate in 2026, they are entering a job market heavily influenced by rapid advances in artificial intelligence. Many experts warn that AI could make some technical skills less relevant, presenting significant challenges for new graduates seeking career opportunities. Students who started college when AI technologies like ChatGPT were not yet mainstream are now confronting a landscape where automation and AI-driven tools are becoming integral to workplaces across industries. Despite these challenges, universities continue to train students in foundational computer science skills, emphasizing adaptability and problem-solving. Career counselors and industry professionals suggest that graduates focus on roles that complement AI, such as human-centered technology development, ethical AI oversight, and interdisciplinary applications. While the headlines might make the situation seem dire, there are opportunities for graduates who are willing

item.title: YouTube launches AI-powered conversational search and integrates Gemini Omni into Shorts
item.description: Google is expanding the role of artificial intelligence on YouTube with the introduction of 'Ask YouTube,' a conversational search tool designed to handle complex queries and follow-ups. The feature allows users to search for detailed advice, creator reviews, and other nuanced content across both long-form videos and Shorts. Currently, Premium subscribers in the U.S. on desktop can access this feature through YouTube's experimental tools. Additionally, Google announced that its AI video model, Gemini Omni, is being added to YouTube Shorts Remix and the YouTube Create app. Gemini Omni aims to enhance content creation by better understanding user intent and improving storytelling, as well as handling complex video and audio adjustments behind the scenes. This rollout comes as other platforms, including Meta and OpenAI, have faced mixed reactions to AI integration in short-form content. YouTube is also expanding its likeness-detection tool for creators aged 18 and older, allowing them to

item.title: Early Wildfires Threaten Homes and Endangered Forests in California
item.description: California is experiencing an unusually active wildfire season earlier than usual, fueled by high winds, heat, and a dry winter. The Santa Rosa Island Fire, sparked by a sailor's distress flares in Channel Islands National Park, has consumed approximately 16,600 acres, threatening a grove of critically endangered Torrey pines. Although initial assessments suggest the grove survived, full evaluations will follow when conditions allow. Meanwhile, the Sandy Fire near Simi Valley has forced thousands to evacuate as it spreads across nearly 1,400 acres, remaining only 5 percent contained, while the River Fire in Kern County has burned 3,535 acres and is 15 percent contained. Across California, nearly 41,000 acres have burned so far, surpassing the five-year average of 23,380 acres for this time of year. Contributing to this early surge is the record-low snowpack in the Sierra Nevada, just 9 percent of the typical level, reflecting a broader Western trend worsened by climate change. Experts

item.title: Trump Endorses Paxton, Raising GOP Concerns Over Senate Control
item.description: Republicans are expressing concern that former President Donald Trump's recent endorsement of Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton could jeopardize their control of the Senate in the upcoming 2026 midterms. Initially, GOP leaders anticipated that Trump would remain neutral in the Texas Senate runoff between Paxton and incumbent Senator John Cornyn. Trump's decision surprised party leadership, who had been hoping he would either endorse Cornyn or stay out of the race entirely. Senators Lindsey Graham and Lisa Murkowski voiced worries that Paxton's candidacy would make the Senate race significantly more expensive and complicated, requiring more fundraising efforts to secure a Republican victory. Senate Majority Leader John Thune reportedly tried to persuade Trump to back Cornyn, but the outcome remained uncertain until Trump's public endorsement. The move has left Republicans recalibrating their strategies, with concerns that Paxton might struggle in the general election and potentially thr

item.title: Historic Magic Show Posters from the Early 20th Century
item.description: During the early 1900s, magic show posters emerged as a unique form of visual storytelling, capturing the imagination of the public and promoting the era's most famous magicians. Artists employed vibrant stone-lithography techniques to create dramatic and eye-catching designs, often featuring supernatural imagery such as floating figures or mischievous devils. These posters were more than advertisements; they conveyed a sense of wonder and mystery, turning performers like Harry Houdini, Howard Thurston, Adelaide Herrmann, and Servais Le Roy into larger-than-life icons. Each poster highlighted a magician’s signature act, from levitations to escape feats, and reflected the artistic trends of the time, including bold typography and rich, saturated colors. Over the years, posters from this 'Golden Age of Magic' have become prized collectibles, offering a glimpse into the entertainment culture of the early 20th century. They not only illustrate the theatrical flair of the magicians but also

item.title: Michael Biehn’s Iconic Role as Kyle Reese in The Terminator
item.description: Michael Biehn's portrayal of Sgt. Kyle Reese in the 1984 film 'The Terminator' is widely recognized as one of the most emotionally compelling performances in science fiction cinema. While Arnold Schwarzenegger’s character represented the unstoppable mechanical force, Biehn brought a human, desperate intensity to the screen. His performance emphasized Reese's background as a survivor of a post-apocalyptic world, constantly alert to danger, which made the film's suspense and stakes feel grounded and real. Biehn’s lean and wiry build reinforced this authenticity, showing a character shaped by famine and guerrilla warfare rather than typical 1980s action hero bulk. The improvised civilian outfit he wore, including a stolen gray trench coat and Nike Vandals, became an iconic look of the era. Interestingly, Biehn nearly lost the role after his initial audition, where a Southern accent he used didn’t fit the character’s persona, but a second audition without the accent secured him the part. D

item.title: Federal Judge Issues Controversial Order Affecting Transgender Minors' Medical Records
item.description: U.S. District Judge Reed O'Connor, appointed by George W. Bush, has issued a highly controversial order directing Rhode Island Hospital to provide private medical records and Social Security numbers of transgender minors who received gender-affirming care. Legal analyst Mark Joseph Stern described the order as a severe abuse of judicial power that could be considered impeachable misconduct. The order also prevents the hospital from seeking relief in Rhode Island courts or assisting others in doing so, raising concerns about federal overreach. This decision comes despite a recent Rhode Island federal judge already rejecting a Justice Department subpoena in a related case, citing misconduct by the DOJ. Stern highlighted that similar subpoenas have been blocked by seven other courts before reaching O'Connor's courtroom in Fort Worth, Texas. Critics argue that this overreach may set up a constitutional conflict regarding the authority of judges aligned with the MAGA movement to affect resi

item.title: Athletics Defeat Angels 14-6 with Strong Offensive Showing from Nick Kurtz, Brent Rooker, and Zack Gelof
item.description: The Oakland Athletics overcame the Los Angeles Angels with a 14-6 victory on Tuesday night in Anaheim, powered by an outstanding performance from Nick Kurtz. Kurtz, the reigning American League Rookie of the Year, recorded three hits and five RBIs, sparking rallies in the third, sixth, and eighth innings. Brent Rooker and Zack Gelof each homered and contributed three RBIs, while reliever Justin Sterner earned the win despite facing a challenging fourth inning. The Athletics scored 12 of their 14 runs with two outs, showcasing their depth and clutch hitting. Mike Trout hit his 12th home run of the season and notched two RBIs for the struggling Angels, who have lost 22 of their last 28 games. Angels starter Reid Detmers allowed eight runs on eight hits over 5 2/3 innings. The Athletics, who are planning to move to Las Vegas in 2028, collected 15 hits overall and snapped a three-game losing streak. The game highlighted Kurtz's ongoing on-base streak, now at 42 games, one of the longest in

item.title: Proposal suggests requiring banks to verify customers’ immigration status amid policy debate
item.description: A report indicates that former U.S. President Donald Trump has proposed or is considering a policy that would require banks to examine or verify the immigration status of their clients. The idea appears to be part of a broader immigration enforcement strategy that could extend compliance responsibilities to financial institutions. Such a move would likely raise significant legal, regulatory, and privacy concerns, as banks are traditionally required to verify identity for anti-money laundering purposes but not immigration status. Financial institutions could face operational challenges in implementing such checks, including determining acceptable documentation, ensuring non-discriminatory practices, and managing potential conflicts with existing federal and state laws. Critics would likely argue that the measure could lead to exclusion of certain groups from the financial system and increase risks of profiling or unequal treatment. Supporters, on the other hand, may frame it as a tool t

item.title: Behind the Scenes of 'The Graduate' with Dustin Hoffman and Katharine Ross
item.description: The 1967 film 'The Graduate' tells the story of Benjamin Braddock, a recent college graduate played by Dustin Hoffman, whose disillusionment with life leads him into a complicated affair with Mrs. Robinson, portrayed by Anne Bancroft. Despite her warnings, Benjamin falls in love with Mrs. Robinson's daughter, Elaine, played by Katharine Ross, ultimately culminating in a dramatic wedding chase that has become one of cinema's iconic moments. Behind the scenes, the chemistry between Hoffman and Ross was intentionally awkward, capturing the uncertain and transformative nature of the characters. Ross initially found Hoffman unkempt and unusually short, but director Mike Nichols used these qualities to enhance the realism of Benjamin's character. The article also features 20 publicity photos from the film’s production, offering fans a glimpse into the making of this classic piece of New Hollywood cinema. These images reveal both the actors’ on-set interactions and the visual style that contr

item.title: Capucine: Icon of French Fashion and Hollywood Cinema
item.description: Capucine, born Germaine Lefebvre, was a celebrated French model and actress who left a lasting mark on both the fashion and film industries. She began her career in Paris as a fashion model, becoming a muse for legendary designers such as Givenchy and Christian Dior. Her aristocratic features and elegant presence earned her the nickname 'ice queen' and caught the attention of Hollywood producers, leading to her breakthrough role in 'Song Without End.' Capucine is perhaps best remembered for her comedic grace as Simone Clouseau in 'The Pink Panther,' but her true legacy lies in her timeless style. She represented a rare combination of understated glamour, French poise, and a subtle vulnerability that captivated audiences and fashion enthusiasts alike. Her work in cinema and modeling made her one of the most iconic faces of the 1960s, influencing both the entertainment world and high fashion. Beyond her roles on screen, Capucine’s persona reflected a sophisticated elegance that continues

item.title: Letter Writer Seeks Advice After Discovering In-Law’s Partner Eating Meat Despite Vegan Claims
item.description: A reader wrote to Carolyn Hax seeking guidance after discovering that their sister-in-law’s partner, James, who presents himself as vegan, was caught eating a burger. James has a reputation for being morally preachy about veganism, often dictating what foods should be served during family gatherings. The letter writer, who hosts holidays for their husband’s side of the family, goes through the effort of planning meals that cater to James’ vegan preferences out of love for their sister-in-law. The incident has left the letter writer frustrated and wondering how to address James’ hypocrisy. The advice column invited readers to share their opinions, offering a range of humorous, practical, and empathetic responses. Many readers highlighted the social awkwardness of managing dietary preferences in family settings, the stress of accommodating someone who publicly enforces moral standards, and strategies for handling such situations diplomatically. Overall, the article explores themes of fam

item.title: Michelle Yeoh's 1998 National Geographic Photo Shoot Above Hollywood
item.description: In 1998, celebrated actress Michelle Yeoh participated in a daring National Geographic photo shoot captured by renowned photographer Joe McNally. The project, part of a feature story titled 'The Globalization of Culture,' explored the rapid spread of cultural icons across the world through movies, television, fashion, and the emerging internet. McNally sought an Asian star capable of performing stunts to highlight the growing influence of Asian actors in mainstream Hollywood cinema. Yeoh, prepared for the shoot, was safely harnessed and rigged under a helicopter while remaining in a hangar before being flown for approximately 20 to 25 minutes toward the Hollywood sign. Although wearing a fashionable, form-fitting dress, Yeoh recalled in a 2022 interview that the cold at that altitude was the most challenging part of the experience. McNally praised Yeoh's magnetic presence in front of the camera and captured numerous striking images, emphasizing both her elegance and the adventurous spi

item.title: State Senate District 48 Primary Results: Candidates Who Will Challenge Incumbent Shawn Still
item.description: The results for the State Senate District 48 primary have been announced, determining the candidate who will compete against incumbent State Senator Shawn Still in the upcoming November elections. The primary election is a crucial step in the political process, as it decides which party candidates will represent their constituencies in the general election. Voters in District 48 had the opportunity to cast their ballots and influence the political landscape in the state Senate. The outcome of this primary provides insights into local political dynamics and voter preferences, shaping the campaign strategies leading up to November. This development is especially significant for constituents of District 48, who will now see a clearer path in the race for representation. Local media and residents have been following the election closely, emphasizing the importance of civic engagement and participation in shaping legislative leadership. The primary's results mark the beginning of intensifie

item.title: Behind the Scenes: Sophia Loren in the 1972 Film ‘Man of La Mancha’
item.description: The 1972 film adaptation of 'Man of La Mancha' remains a visually striking exploration of idealism, combining a theatrical 'play-within-a-play' approach with a historical setting in a Spanish Inquisition dungeon. The movie features Peter O’Toole in a dual role as Miguel de Cervantes and his iconic creation, Don Quixote. Sophia Loren delivers a compelling performance as Aldonza, capturing the emotional depth of her character and leaving a lasting impression. Despite some criticism regarding the adaptation from stage to screen, the film’s rendition of 'The Impossible Dream' stands out as a memorable musical highlight. These behind-the-scenes portraits of Sophia Loren provide a rare glimpse into the filming process, revealing the actress’s dedication and charisma on set. The photos also serve as a nostalgic window into 1970s cinema, showcasing the artistry and ambition of filmmakers during that era. Overall, the story is not only a tribute to the classic literary work but also a meditatio

item.title: Photographs Documenting the Assassination of Robert F. Kennedy in 1968
item.description: On June 5, 1968, Senator Robert F. Kennedy, a leading Democratic presidential candidate, was mortally wounded by Sirhan Sirhan at the Ambassador Hotel in Los Angeles, California. Kennedy had just won the California and South Dakota primaries and addressed his supporters before exiting through a kitchen hallway, where he was shot. He died nearly 25 hours later at Good Samaritan Hospital and was buried at Arlington National Cemetery. Sirhan, a Palestinian with anti-Zionist views, confessed to the shooting and was initially sentenced to death, later commuted to life imprisonment. The assassination of Kennedy led to the implementation of Secret Service protection for presidential candidates and spurred various conspiracy theories. Bill Eppridge captured photographs before, during, and after the assassination, which reflect the fear, grief, and tension of the moment. These images also reveal the admiration and hope Kennedy inspired among his supporters. The tragedy underscores the unfulfill

item.title: Washington Post Editorial Board Argues Trump's Primary Win Over Rep. Thomas Massie Could Hurt Republicans in Midterms
item.description: President Donald Trump achieved a personal win on Tuesday when Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY), a frequent critic within the GOP, lost his primary to MAGA-endorsed candidate Ed Gallrein, a former Navy SEAL. However, The Washington Post editorial board suggested this victory might prove costly for the Republican Party overall. The board described it as a potential 'Pyrrhic victory' that could harm the GOP in the upcoming 2026 midterms. According to the editorial, Trump's strong influence is making vulnerable Republican incumbents in competitive districts hesitant to distance themselves from him, even as his approval ratings remain low with the broader electorate. This fear of primary challenges or backlash from Trump supporters is discouraging lawmakers from taking independent stances. The Post acknowledged Massie's efforts to control national debt but criticized his limited effectiveness as a 'gadfly,' his solitary vote against a resolution condemning antisemitism, and what they called consp

item.title: ArchGanics: Exploring Carlos Antón Varó's Futuristic Curved Living Spaces
item.description: Spanish digital artist Carlos Antón Varó has created a series of architectural renderings titled ArchGanics, featuring surreal and abstract sculpted living quarters. The designs showcase smooth, flowing curves, built-in planters, and oval skylights, all contributing to a sense of calm and otherworldliness. Varó describes these spaces as 'idealized places where the mind would love to stay,' which reflects the immersive and meditative quality of his work. The series evokes futuristic aesthetics reminiscent of science fiction environments, with comparisons made to stylish beach bungalows on Alderaan from Star Wars. ArchGanics emphasizes a harmonious integration of nature and architecture, with each space carefully designed to feel serene, organized, and visually engaging. These digital renderings highlight the potential of architectural imagination to create environments that inspire tranquility, creativity, and a sense of escape from conventional living spaces. The project has been showc

item.title: Google introduces Gmail Live: AI-powered voice assistant for your inbox
item.description: At Google IO 2026, Google unveiled a major upgrade to Gmail with the introduction of Gmail Live, a new feature powered by Gemini AI. Gmail Live allows users to interact with their inbox using natural language, enabling them to ask questions about emails, appointments, travel details, and tasks without typing search terms. For instance, users can ask about upcoming flights, hotel information, or school events, and Gmail Live can provide detailed responses, including specifics like hotel room numbers. The AI can also follow conversations, handle interruptions, and switch topics naturally. Google emphasized that Gmail Live complements traditional search rather than replacing it, learning from past backlash against AI-only experiences in Google Photos. Additional features include ready-to-send drafts, instant file access, and enhanced task management through Google AI Ultra, Pro, and Plus subscriptions. While the voice-powered Gmail Live will initially be exclusive to Ultra subscribers, Go

item.title: Rene Russo’s Vogue Covers and Modeling Career in the 1970s
item.description: Before becoming a renowned Hollywood actress, Rene Russo was one of the most prominent supermodels of the 1970s. Discovered at a Rolling Stones concert at just 17, she quickly became a favorite subject for legendary photographers such as Francesco Scavullo and Richard Avedon. Throughout the mid-1970s, Russo frequently appeared on magazine covers, sharing the spotlight with other icons like Patti Hansen and Rosie Vela. Her Vogue covers showcased her versatility, from glamorous high-fashion portraits with decadent furs and bold makeup to approachable, aspirational beach shoots. Francesco Scavullo, in particular, captured her iconic 'big hair' looks and high-glamour style. Vogue editors often praised Russo for her ability to adapt to diverse editorial demands with a mix of elegance and tenacity. By the late 1970s and early 1980s, as the supermodel era evolved, Russo transitioned to acting and eventually retired from modeling, embarking on the film career for which she is now well known. T

item.title: Trump Threatens to Withdraw Endorsement of Lauren Boebert Over Massie Support, But Primary Deadline Has Passed
item.description: President Donald Trump has publicly threatened to withdraw his endorsement of U.S. Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-CO) and support a primary challenger after she campaigned for Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) in his primary election. Boebert joined Massie at events in Kentucky last week, despite Trump backing a challenger against Massie. Massie ultimately lost his primary on Tuesday. Trump posted on Truth Social criticizing Boebert as 'Weak Minded' for her support of Massie, who had pushed for the release of Epstein files. He stated it would be his honor to endorse an alternative if the right person emerges. Boebert responded on X, saying she was not offended and understood the risks, reaffirming her commitment to America First and MAGA principles. However, it is too late for any challenger to enter the June 30 Colorado Republican primary ballot, which has already been certified. Ballots have been mailed to overseas voters, and Boebert is running unopposed. Write-in candidates for the general electio

item.title: Recovered Photographs Reveal the Tragic Fate of Andrée's 1897 Arctic Balloon Expedition
item.description: In 1897, Swedish engineer S. A. Andrée led a daring expedition to reach the North Pole using a hydrogen balloon called The Eagle. Accompanied by engineer Knut Frænkel and photographer Nils Strindberg, the mission was funded by King Oscar II and Alfred Nobel, aiming to claim the Pole for Sweden through innovative aerial exploration. Shortly after takeoff from Svalbard on July 11, the balloon lost its heavy steering ropes, leaving it at the mercy of the wind. It traveled only 65 hours before a controlled emergency landing on Arctic pack ice. The men survived the landing but were unprepared for the harsh terrain. They undertook a difficult three-month trek south, eventually reaching Kvitøya, where they perished. The fate of the expedition remained unknown for 33 years until Norwegian whalers discovered the final camp in 1930, remarkably preserved by the Arctic cold. Diaries and photographic film were recovered, providing an unprecedented glimpse into the expedition’s final months. Modern

item.title: A Glimpse of Life in 1980s Corfu Through Vintage Photos
item.description: The 1980s were a transformative period for Corfu, Greece, as the island evolved from a quiet Mediterranean getaway to a popular destination for European tourists. This decade witnessed the rise of the package holiday, bringing waves of visitors to Corfu Town and the scenic hills of Kavos, along with the hum of mopeds, lively disco music, and bustling summer energy. Despite the increasing tourism, Corfu maintained its rustic charm, offering long summer nights, the scent of wild sage and sea air, and a warm local hospitality that stayed genuine amid the growing influx of visitors. These vintage photographs, captured by Paul Clarke during his parents' trip, provide a window into everyday life, showing how locals and tourists coexisted in a time of unfiltered freedom. The images illustrate both the cultural shift and enduring traditions, capturing the island’s beauty, vibrancy, and character during a pivotal decade. Corfu in the 1980s was a blend of leisure, music, and Mediterranean charm,

item.title: Irish Government Investigates Potentially Suspicious Bets on Dublin Central By-Election
item.description: The Irish Government has announced that Gardaí, regulators, and various government departments will investigate suspicious betting activity on the Polymarket platform, particularly surrounding the recent Dublin Central by-election. Tánaiste Simon Harris expressed serious concerns about the rapid growth of unregulated betting, describing it as a 'Wild West' environment where cryptocurrency is used in a secretive and murky way. Reports suggest that around one million dollars were wagered on the election, with hundreds of thousands specifically betting against candidate Gerard Hutch. Harris emphasized that there is no suggestion of wrongdoing by any candidate, but the scale and nature of the bets raise questions about potential money laundering and the influence of foreign participants. The investigation will involve collaboration between the Department of Justice, An Garda Síochána, the gambling regulator, the Central Bank, EU partners, and other relevant agencies. Harris stressed the im

item.title: Wall Street Journal Criticizes Trump's $1.8 Billion Fund Deal Amid Legal and Ethical Concerns
item.description: The Wall Street Journal's editorial board sharply criticized former President Donald Trump's recent $1.8 billion anti-weaponization fund deal, calling it a 'rotten' agreement that reflects the current distorted political climate. The fund was created as part of a settlement following a lawsuit against the IRS, and it includes additional protections for Trump himself. While the Trump family is ineligible to receive payouts, the WSJ highlighted that some January 6, 2021, rioters might be able to benefit from the fund. The editorial suggested that these participants could claim they were wrongly accused or that police actions were responsible for injuries during the Capitol riots. Federal Judge Kathleen Williams, who oversaw the lawsuit, expressed uncertainty over whether she had jurisdiction since Trump was president and had oversight of the IRS. A hearing is scheduled for May 27 to clarify this jurisdictional question. Meanwhile, reports indicate that the Justice Department does not bel

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law resigns from Trump administration intelligence roles
item.description: Amaryllis Fox Kennedy, daughter-in-law of U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is stepping down from her two senior intelligence roles in the Trump administration. Kennedy has been a key ally of Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard and held influential positions within national security agencies. Reports suggest her resignation may be partly due to disagreements with President Trump's military policies regarding Iran, echoing a similar reason cited by Joe Kent, former director of the National Counterterrorism Center, who resigned earlier this year. In a May 8 email to colleagues, Kennedy stated that her departure is to return to the private sector and prioritize her family, emphasizing the importance of motherhood. She did not mention political disagreements in the email, which included praise for Trump. Kennedy's resignation is set to take effect this Friday, and it comes amid broader concerns within the administration about Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'

item.title: Historical Portraits of Women Involved in New York's Mafia
item.description: Throughout much of the 20th century, New York City’s organized crime scene was dominated by infamous figures such as Lucky Luciano, Bugsy Siegel, Meyer Lansky, and Frank Costello. While the men of the Mafia have been extensively documented, the women who lived alongside them—often called mob molls, gun molls, or gangster molls—have received far less attention. Derived from the 17th-century term “Molly,” meaning prostitute, the term evolved in New York to describe women who were intimately connected to the city’s criminal underworld. These women were not merely passive companions; many played active roles in criminal operations, acting as couriers, intelligence gatherers, and intermediaries between crime families. Figures like Virginia Hill, dubbed the “queen of the gangster molls,” and Janice Drake, a former beauty queen, illustrate the range of involvement and danger these women faced. Others, like Alice Granville and Margo Donohue, experienced direct violence and legal repercussions,

item.title: Anjelica Huston: A Legendary Career in Hollywood
item.description: Anjelica Huston, born into Hollywood royalty as the daughter of famed director John Huston, has established herself as one of the most compelling actresses of her generation. Known for her aristocratic features, commanding presence, and subtle yet powerful performances, Huston transitioned seamlessly from high-fashion modeling to award-winning acting. Her career is highlighted by a range of roles that showcase her versatility, from the macabre elegance of Morticia Addams in The Addams Family to the gritty intensity of Prizzi’s Honor. She has also frequently collaborated with Wes Anderson, demonstrating a distinctive deadpan charm that has become part of her signature style. Beyond her cinematic achievements, Huston is celebrated as a symbol of unconventional beauty and independence, commanding attention without raising her voice. Her enduring influence in Hollywood reflects not only her talent but also her dedication to portraying complex, memorable female characters, making her a true

item.title: A Retrospective on Robert Duvall’s Iconic 1970s Film Roles
item.description: Robert Duvall, one of Hollywood’s most respected actors, passed away peacefully at his home in Middleburg, Virginia, on February 15, 2026, at the age of 95. Known for his exceptional talent in both leading and supporting roles, Duvall left an indelible mark on 1970s cinema. During this decade, he became a key figure in the New Hollywood movement, gaining acclaim for his gritty and realistic acting style. His performances as Tom Hagen in The Godfather (1972) and The Godfather Part II (1974) showcased his calm, authoritative presence and earned him his first Academy Award nomination. He also had an uncredited but pivotal role in The Conversation (1974), adding depth to the film's atmosphere of paranoia. Duvall’s portrayal of Lt. Col. Bill Kilgore in Apocalypse Now (1979) brought him another Oscar nomination and a Golden Globe, cementing his status as a cinematic icon. Beyond Coppola’s films, Duvall excelled at playing complex, often intimidating characters, from Major Frank Burns in M*A*

item.title: Review: Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight Combines Lego Humor with Arkham-Inspired Gameplay
item.description: Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight is TT Games’ latest entry into the Lego Batman universe, blending RPG, brawler, and classic Lego platforming elements into a fun, family-friendly adventure. The game pays homage to the Batman: Arkham series with a new combat system, environmental takedowns, and combo mechanics, though it remains simpler and more accessible than its Arkham inspirations. Players control a smaller roster of seven characters, each with unique skills, while exploring a highly detailed, lively Gotham City filled with collectibles, hidden loot, and Easter eggs referencing decades of Batman media. The game’s humor and parodic take on iconic scenes make it an entertaining experience, though some glitches and performance demands, particularly on handheld devices, slightly detract from gameplay. Three difficulty settings, including options without fail states, make it suitable for a wide range of players, and comprehensive accessibility options cover vision, hearing, motor,

item.title: Commonwealth Short Story Prize Winners Under Scrutiny for Possible AI Use
item.description: The 2026 Commonwealth Short Story Prize winners have come under intense scrutiny after allegations emerged that several of the winning stories may have been generated, wholly or partially, using artificial intelligence. Three of the five regional winners—Jamir Nazir from the Caribbean, John Edward DeMicoli from Canada and Europe, and Sharon Aruparayil from Asia—have been flagged by AI-detection tool Pangram as fully or partially AI-generated. While Nazir’s story “The Serpent in the Grove” has raised questions about its style and metaphors, the Commonwealth Foundation maintains that all submissions are considered original and judged without AI tools. Both the Foundation and Granta, which hosts the published winning stories, have emphasized the current unreliability of AI-detection software for unpublished fiction and have called for trust in the authors’ declarations. Despite this, the controversy reflects a broader tension in literature and academia as AI tools increasingly intersect w

item.title: JD Vance deflects questions on Trump's stock trading amid ethics concerns
item.description: Vice President JD Vance faced questions on Tuesday regarding President Donald Trump's stock trading practices, which were highlighted in recent financial disclosures. The disclosures revealed that Trump traded stocks in companies he publicly promoted at White House events and on social media, sometimes including ticker symbols to encourage purchases. Reporters questioned the apparent conflict between the administration's anti-corruption messaging and Trump's personal financial gains, suggesting potential insider trading. Vance did not directly address the allegations, instead criticizing the reporter's approach to questioning. He maintained that Trump relies on independent wealth advisors and does not personally execute trades, calling claims of insider trading 'absurd.' Vance also reiterated support for banning congressional stock trading, stating the administration intends to make such trades illegal, but he did not clarify why similar rules would not apply to the president. Public o

item.title: Republican opposition grows against $1B White House ballroom funding
item.description: Funding for President Donald Trump's planned White House ballroom, which is part of a broader East Wing modernization project, is facing increasing opposition from within the Republican Party. While the ballroom itself has been presented as a $400 million privately funded initiative, a separate $1 billion request for taxpayer-funded security measures tied to the project has drawn significant criticism. Several Republican senators, including Bill Cassidy, Susan Collins, Lisa Murkowski, and outgoing Thom Tillis, have publicly opposed the funding, citing concerns over the lack of planning and inflated costs. Politico reports that the project is 'on the brink of being ejected' from the GOP's immigration legislation, reflecting both public objections and private dissent among party members. The controversy intensified after a Senate parliamentarian ruled that the $1 billion allocation could not be included under reconciliation rules, prompting Trump to demand the firing of the parliamentari

item.title: Edmund Adler: Capturing the Innocence of Childhood in Art
item.description: Edmund Adler (1876–1965) was an Austrian genre painter celebrated for his tender and detailed depictions of childhood and rural life. Trained at the Vienna Academy, Adler had a remarkable ability to portray the innocence, curiosity, and joy of children in everyday settings. His paintings often feature children playing with pets, sharing meals, or exploring the natural world, rendered with exquisite technical precision and soft, natural light that gives his works a timeless and nostalgic quality. Adler's art avoids the grand historical or dramatic themes of his time, instead emphasizing quiet, harmonious moments of peasant life. His paintings, such as 'A Little Tragedy,' 'Her Favorite Puppy,' and 'The Little Rider,' reflect an enduring humanity and warmth that resonates with viewers who appreciate realism and intimacy. Adler’s work remains a valuable window into early 20th-century life and the simple joys of childhood, offering both historical insight and emotional connection. Through h

item.title: Sen. Cassidy votes to advance bill limiting Trump's war powers following primary defeat
item.description: Senator Bill Cassidy of Louisiana cast a surprising procedural vote in the Senate to advance legislation that would limit former President Donald Trump's authority over military actions in Iran. This move came shortly after Cassidy suffered a high-profile primary election loss, where Trump had endorsed his opponent. The bill, aimed at curbing presidential war powers, requires the President to seek congressional approval for military engagements or withdraw troops. Cassidy, who had previously been targeted by Trump for his vote to impeach him following the January 6 Capitol events, joined all Democrats and three other Republican senators—Lisa Murkowski, Susan Collins, and Rand Paul—in supporting the motion. The procedural vote passed 50-47, moving the legislation to the next stage, although it does not yet enact the restrictions. Cassidy finished third in the Louisiana primary behind Rep. Julia Letlow and State Treasurer John Fleming, who will advance to a runoff. This vote highlights C

item.title: Japan Airlines Flight 2 Makes Emergency Water Landing in San Francisco Bay in 1968
item.description: On November 22, 1968, Japan Air Lines Flight 2, a Douglas DC-8-62 named Shiga, accidentally landed in the shallow waters of San Francisco Bay, approximately 2.5 miles short of San Francisco International Airport. The flight, originating from Tokyo, encountered thick fog and low visibility, which led Captain Kohei Asoh to mistakenly believe the aircraft was over the runway. Miraculously, all 107 passengers and crew survived without injury. The unusually high tide cushioned the impact, keeping exit doors above the waterline and allowing for a safe evacuation. Passengers were assisted by lifeboats and transported to Coyote Point Yacht Harbor by the Coast Guard and local authorities. Captain Asoh’s extraordinary accountability during the NTSB investigation, famously stating, “As you Americans say, I f---ed up,” became known as the “Asoh Defense,” an example of radical honesty in leadership. Rather than being fired, Asoh was demoted and retrained, eventually returning to fly for JAL until r

item.title: Funding Cuts Under Trump Administration Implicated in Ebola Outbreak in DRC
item.description: A recent report highlights that funding reductions under the Trump administration have left parts of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) vulnerable to a rare strain of Ebola, now infecting over 500 people. The International Rescue Committee (IRC) stated that the loss of support in March 2025 forced them to scale back early-warning systems designed to detect the virus. Heather Reoch Kerr, the IRC's country director in Congo, explained that the recent spike in reported cases reflects infections that may have been occurring for some time, only now being detected due to partial restoration of surveillance efforts. The outbreak involves the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, a rare variant for which there is no licensed vaccine or targeted treatment. The World Health Organization (WHO) declared the outbreak a public health emergency and expressed concern about its rapid spread. The IRC confirmed that health and preparedness operations were halted in three areas of Ituri Province, which is c

item.title: Funding Cuts Threaten Veterans Treatment Courts Supporting Rehabilitation
item.description: Veterans in the U.S. face significant challenges after military service, including mental health issues, substance use disorders, homelessness, and incarceration. Approximately 8% of all Americans in prisons or jails are veterans, often due to these struggles. Veterans Treatment Courts (VTCs) have been a key tool in addressing these problems for over 15 years. Modeled after drug treatment courts, VTCs offer veterans accused or convicted of crimes a chance to address the root causes of their behavior rather than simply serving jail time. These courts provide access to specialized clinicians, counseling, housing support, and other social services, often leveraging benefits from the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA). Operating in over 745 courthouses across nearly all U.S. states, VTCs require veterans to participate in regular hearings, drug tests, therapy, and community engagement. Successful completion can lead to reduced charges, fines, or probation termination. Studies suggest thes

item.title: U.S. Mint Delays Trump Commemorative Gold Coin Beyond July 4, 2026
item.description: The U.S. Mint has announced that it will not be able to produce gold coins featuring President Donald Trump in time for the United States' 250th birthday. According to a legal filing by April Stafford, director of the Mint's Office of Design Management, the coin is still in the design phase and has not yet received final approval. The Treasury Department is reviewing the design and may request modifications, which could further delay production. Once the design is approved, it will take six to eight weeks to begin minting the coins, with additional months required to complete production. While the coin is intended to commemorate the Semiquincentennial on July 4, 2026, the release date has not been set, and estimates suggest the coins may not be available until December 2026 or even early 2027. This update clarifies that the coins will not coincide with the actual birthday celebrations, highlighting logistical and administrative challenges at the Mint in producing high-value commemorati

item.title: The Espionage and Unusual Romance of Shi Pei Pu and Bernard Boursicot
item.description: Shi Pei Pu (1938–2009), a Chinese opera singer from Beijing, was at the center of one of the most unusual espionage cases of the 20th century. In 1964, he met Bernard Boursicot, a 20-year-old French diplomat, and convinced him that he was a woman disguised as a man due to family pressure. The two began a romantic and sexual relationship that lasted for 20 years. Shi maintained the deception through extreme modesty and manipulation, even claiming to be pregnant and presenting a child as their own, though the child was adopted. During their affair, Chinese intelligence coerced Boursicot into providing embassy documents, resulting in the transfer of around 500 classified documents to China between 1977 and 1982. The truth emerged in 1983 when the pair moved to Paris, and medical examinations confirmed Shi was biologically male. Both were convicted of espionage in 1986 and sentenced to six years in prison but were pardoned in 1987, with Shi returning to opera. Despite the deception, Shi an

item.title: Philadelphia Establishes Ona Judge Day to Commemorate the Courage of Martha Washington’s Enslaved Maid
item.description: On May 21, 1796, Ona Judge, an enslaved woman owned by Martha Washington, took a daring step to secure her freedom. Working as a chambermaid in the President’s House in Philadelphia, Judge was responsible for grueling daily tasks, including bathing, dressing, and attending to Martha Washington’s family. Aware of her impending transfer to Martha’s granddaughter in Virginia, Judge planned and executed a quiet escape to New Hampshire, where she could live freely. Her escape took place while the Washingtons dined, and she relied on friends among Philadelphia’s free Black community to assist her. Philadelphia, a city where an estimated 5% to 9% of the population were free Black people, provided Judge with a glimpse of liberty. In recognition of her bravery, the city of Philadelphia has established May 21 as Ona Judge Day. The day honors her courage and highlights the broader history of slavery in the United States, which has faced efforts to be suppressed in public memory. The National Park

item.title: Investigation Finds Military-Grade Communications at Epstein's Former Ranch
item.description: Journalist Alisa Valdes-Rodríguez examined Justice Department files and reported that Jeffrey Epstein's Zorro Ranch in New Mexico featured advanced communications infrastructure potentially linked to U.S. intelligence. Valdes-Rodríguez identified military-grade equipment, including a private microwave communications link, still maintained by current owner Don Huffines, an ally of former President Donald Trump. The property was likely constructed by Bradbury Stamm, a contractor known for government projects, rather than typical residential construction. A 2016 email showed Epstein opting for highly secure, industrial-grade communication systems designed to prevent interception. These findings build on prior reporting suggesting Epstein may have had connections to the CIA from a young age, possibly with the involvement of Donald Barr, father of William Barr. No official confirmation has been provided by the CIA or the Justice Department. While these discoveries raise questions about Epst

item.title: Former MAGA Influencer Claims Elon Musk Had Early Knowledge of 2024 Election Results
item.description: Ashley St. Clair, a former MAGA influencer who has publicly distanced herself from her previous political views, claims that Tesla CEO Elon Musk informed her about President Donald Trump's victory in the 2024 election hours before it was officially reported by the Associated Press. According to St. Clair, Musk has access to 'real-time' election data through undisclosed satellite technology. She also alleges that Musk shared internal data from America PAC with her, raising questions about how he obtained election information beyond standard campaign methods. St. Clair became publicly known after announcing that she had given birth to one of Musk's 14 children, noting that Musk allegedly limited child support and spent minimal time with the child during its first year. Her statements about Musk’s knowledge and personal behavior emerged following a falling out between the two, alongside her renouncement of previous conservative rhetoric. These allegations have sparked discussions about pr

item.title: Street Life in 1950s New York City Through Nick DeWolf’s Photography
item.description: The article explores New York City in the late 1950s through the lens of American photographer Nick DeWolf. During this era, the city was a dynamic blend of modernity and grit, with bustling streets filled with chrome Cadillacs, yellow Checker Cabs, and sharply dressed pedestrians. Iconic landmarks such as Grand Central Terminal, the New York Public Library, the Empire State Building, and Times Square were captured in vivid detail, reflecting the cultural and architectural vibrancy of the time. DeWolf’s photos also highlight the contrast between the working-class neighborhoods like the Bowery and the soaring optimism of newly constructed skyscrapers, exemplified by the Seagram Building. Beyond urban landscapes, the photographer documented scenes in airports such as LaGuardia and Idlewild, as well as everyday social settings, including diners, bars, and theaters. These images offer a unique window into mid-century New York, showing the rhythm, energy, and human stories that defined the

item.title: Jonathan Andic Detained Amid Investigation into Father Isak Andic's Death
item.description: Jonathan Andic, 45, the son of Mango founder Isak Andic, has been arrested by Catalan police in connection with his father's death. Isak Andic, 71, passed away in December 2024 after falling from a ravine in the Montserrat mountains near Barcelona while walking with his son. The incident was initially treated as an accident, but the investigation was later reopened due to inconsistencies in Jonathan's testimony. Conflicting reports exist regarding whether other family members were present during the fall. Jonathan, who joined Mango in 2005 and later oversaw the Mango Man line, became vice-chairman of the board after his father’s passing. During his questioning, Jonathan denied any responsibility for the incident. He has been released on bail set at €1 million (£865,000), with his passport confiscated and a requirement to report weekly to the court. Isak Andic co-founded Mango with his brother Nahman in 1984 in Barcelona, and the brand now operates nearly 3,000 stores in 120 countries.

item.title: Google Launches Pics, an AI Design Tool for Workspace Users
item.description: At its 2026 Google I/O event, Google introduced Pics, a new AI-powered design and image-generation tool integrated into Google Workspace. Pics aims to make visual creation accessible to a broad range of users, from teachers to small business owners, without requiring advanced design skills. Users can generate social media graphics, invitations, marketing materials, and mock-ups through simple text prompts. Beyond image generation, Pics allows precise editing of individual elements using Google’s Gemini-powered editing layer, making modifications intuitive and collaborative, similar to commenting in Google Docs. The tool is powered by Nano Banana 2, supporting detailed visual outputs, accurate text rendering, and real-world knowledge. Pics is designed for collaborative workflows, allowing designs to be downloaded, shared, or passed to others for additional edits. Google positions Pics as a competitor to existing AI-native design platforms like Canva and Anthropic’s Claude Design, signal

item.title: Medical Examiner Rules Death of 12-Year-Old California Girl Due to Natural Causes Despite Head Injury
item.description: Khimberly Zavaleta Chuquipa, a 12-year-old student at Reseda Charter High School, died days after being hit in the head by a metal water bottle during an alleged bullying incident. Despite initial reports linking the injury to her hospitalization and subsequent death, the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner determined that the cause of death was natural, specifically a spontaneous rupture of blood vessels caused by a congenital cerebral arteriovenous malformation (AVM). This rare condition involves fragile, tangled arteries in the brain that can rupture suddenly and catastrophically. Khimberly’s family disputes the ruling, arguing that she was a healthy, vibrant child prior to the incident and that the injury contributed to her fatal brain bleed. The family has also filed a lawsuit against the Los Angeles Unified School District, claiming repeated failures to address bullying on campus. Previously, a juvenile suspect had been arrested on suspicion of murder, though the medical examiner
