item.title: Portrait of William Casby, a Formerly Enslaved American, With His Great-Great-Granddaughter
item.description: In 1963, iconic photographer Richard Avedon captured a striking portrait of William Casby, a man born in 1857 who had experienced slavery firsthand, holding his infant great-great-granddaughter, Cherri Stamps-McCray. Taken on March 24, 1963, in Algiers, Louisiana, the photograph symbolizes a remarkable span of American history. Casby, who later worked as a longshoreman, lived to the age of 113, passing away in 1970. Avedon’s photograph, part of his collaboration with writer James Baldwin for the book Nothing Personal (1964), strips away all background to focus intensely on Casby’s presence, emphasizing the depth of his age, the texture of his skin, and the strength of his hands. Critics and historians, including Roland Barthes, have praised the image for its ability to collapse time and serve as a visual bridge between generations. Taken just months before the historic March on Washington, the photo reminds viewers that the legacy of slavery was still alive and relevant. It combines hi

item.title: Trump Administration Faces Questions Over $1.7 Billion Fund Linked to Jan. 6 Cases
item.description: The Trump administration and U.S. Attorney for D.C., Jeanine Pirro, admitted Monday that they have little information about a newly announced $1.7 billion “anti-weaponization fund.” The fund was created as part of a legal settlement in which former President Trump dropped a $10 billion lawsuit against the federal government related to his taxes, classified documents, and election-related claims. Reports indicate that the fund could potentially be used to provide financial support to individuals convicted of participating in the January 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol, although the details remain unclear. When asked, Trump said he was not involved in creating or negotiating the fund and that distribution decisions would be made by a committee of “respected and brilliant” individuals, whose identities have not been disclosed. Pirro also expressed unfamiliarity with the fund, appearing unaware of its purpose and distancing herself from any involvement. The announcement has drawn criti

item.title: Scott Jennings expresses concern over Trump’s $1.776 billion DOJ fund for allies
item.description: Scott Jennings, a longtime GOP political consultant and CNN commentator, voiced concerns about a newly established $1.776 billion fund linked to former President Donald Trump. The fund, part of a $10 billion settlement in a lawsuit against the IRS, is intended to compensate Trump's allies who claim they were unfairly prosecuted under the Biden administration. Jennings highlighted two main issues: the large sum of money bypassed congressional approval, and the potential for the president to selectively reward political allies, raising questions about fairness and accountability. According to reporting, the Attorney General will appoint a five-member commission overseeing payments, with Trump having the ability to remove members at will. Additionally, the government cannot be held liable for crimes committed by recipients of the funds. Jennings also criticized the idea of providing payments to individuals involved in violent acts during the January 6 insurrection, stating that those conv

item.title: Muhammad Shehada Discusses Ongoing Challenges and Resilience of Palestinians in Gaza
item.description: On Nakba Day, Palestinians around the world reflect on the forced displacement that occurred 78 years ago during the establishment of the state of Israel. Muhammad Shehada, a Palestinian writer originally from Gaza, highlights that the process of Nakba continues today, especially after the events of October 7, 2023. He explains that Israeli policies have perpetuated oppression and violence against Palestinians, framing the situation as a long-term struggle to undermine their political autonomy. Shehada critiques what he calls the 'disarmament trap,' where Israeli negotiations pressure Palestinians to surrender and become defenseless, placing them at greater risk. He also comments on the blockade and occupation of Gaza, describing the daily challenges faced by its residents. Furthermore, Shehada responds to the Israeli government’s recent threat against The New York Times for publishing allegations of systemic abuse of Palestinian detainees, calling it an overreaction to reporting on hu

item.title: The Friendship of James Dean and Elizabeth Taylor on the Set of 'Giant'
item.description: During the filming of the 1955 movie 'Giant' in Marfa, Texas, James Dean and Elizabeth Taylor developed a profound and memorable friendship. Both young stars at the time, they bonded over late-night conversations in Taylor’s trailer and the El Paisano Hotel, sharing their personal struggles and vulnerabilities. Dean, known for his Method acting, confided to Taylor about his childhood trauma, while Taylor, an MGM studio star, often acted as a bridge between Dean and co-star Rock Hudson, who had conflicts with Dean on set. Their friendship, marked by mutual support and understanding, was tragically cut short when Dean died in a car accident just as the Texas filming wrapped up. Taylor was deeply affected by his death and reportedly hospitalized due to grief. Despite the emotional toll, she was required to continue filming immediately, acting in scenes that had originally involved Dean. Photographs from this period capture candid, intimate moments of the two stars and remain iconic images

item.title: CBS News Leadership May Shift Amid Controversy Over Bari Weiss' Management
item.description: Bari Weiss, a conservative-leaning journalist, has faced significant challenges since taking the helm at CBS News, with low ratings and a series of controversies marking her tenure. Despite support from Paramount-Skydance head David Ellison, who is known for his pro-Trump stance, there are discussions within senior leadership about potentially limiting Weiss' control over CBS' major programs such as Evening News, CBS Mornings, and 60 Minutes. Reports suggest that while Weiss may maintain broad editorial influence, day-to-day operations could be handed to a more experienced executive. This comes after several high-profile incidents, including Weiss delaying a '60 Minutes' story about a foreign detention facility and threatening legal action against a reporter. Although no final decisions have been made, potential candidates for the role include current or former executives within CNN or outside hires. The ongoing situation highlights the tension between Weiss' editorial vision and manag

item.title: The Enduring Legacy of Michael Chanda, Master Milliner
item.description: Michael Chanda (1920–2004), a Czechoslovakia-born milliner who grew up in the United States, became a prominent figure in mid-20th-century fashion through his innovative approach to hat design. After three formative years at Hattie Carnegie's salon and work with New York theatrical designers, Chanda launched his own business in 1944. He specialized in small, elegant hats that catered to women of all ages, recognizing the need for designs that complemented petite frames and offered comfort without sacrificing style. His hats were widely celebrated for their transformative qualities, making wearers appear youthful and sophisticated. Chanda's creations were featured in top fashion magazines like Vogue, Harper's Bazaar, and Junior Bazaar, often styled alongside designers such as Charles James, Jo Copeland, and Carolyn. His work was worn by celebrated models and actresses including Dorian Leigh, Inga Lindgren, Marilyn Ambrose, and Carmen Dell’Orefice. Throughout the 1940s and 1950s, Chanda'

item.title: Ella Langley Dominates ACM Awards 2026; Cody Johnson Wins Entertainer of the Year
item.description: The 2026 Academy of Country Music Awards, held at the MGM Grand Garden Arena in Las Vegas and hosted by Shania Twain, celebrated the biggest names in country music. The night was dominated by rising star Ella Langley, who won all seven of her nominations across five categories, including Female Artist of the Year, Song of the Year, and Single of the Year for her hit 'Choosin’ Texas.' Miranda Lambert co-wrote and co-produced the track. Cody Johnson earned the prestigious Entertainer of the Year award, while Parker McCollum took home Album of the Year. Other notable performances included Kacey Musgraves performing her new single 'Dry Spell' and Blake Shelton closing the night with a tribute to the late songwriter Don Schlitz. Emotional highlights included Dan + Shay performing 'Say So' in honor of music executive Ben Vaughn, with Lauren Alaina introducing the performance and advocating for mental health awareness. Duo of the Year was awarded to Brooks & Dunn, and Riley Green alongside La

item.title: Atlantic columnist questions media scrutiny of Trump's age compared to Biden
item.description: An article in The Atlantic, highlighted by Fox News, claims that President Donald Trump's age and stamina are not receiving the same level of scrutiny that former President Joe Biden faced. Jonathan Lemire wrote that when Trump assumed office at 78, he became the oldest president to start a term, yet as he approaches 80, his age is reportedly not being questioned as closely despite signs like reduced travel and appearing tired during meetings. The piece also notes that Biden's age and abilities were initially downplayed by the White House, with media dismissing concerns as 'right-wing talking points' until his debate performance in 2024 exposed weaknesses. Critics argue that the media previously ignored signs of Biden's decline, labeling viral videos as 'cheap fakes.' White House spokesperson Davis Ingle defended Trump's health and energy, contrasting it with what they describe as media coverage that concealed Biden's mental and physical decline. Commentators on social media criticized

item.title: Rachel Maddow Highlights Potential Conflicts in Trump's Stock Trades and Public Statements
item.description: During her show, Rachel Maddow focused on President Donald Trump's recent financial disclosures, pointing out a concerning pattern in his stock trades. Maddow highlighted that several trades coincided with significant policy announcements that had the potential to impact markets. One example she cited was a January purchase of stock in a major microchip company just before Trump relaxed regulations allowing the company to operate in China. Another instance involved Trump's purchase of $9 million in Dell stock, followed by public encouragement to rally attendees to buy Dell shares. Maddow also mentioned Trump's promotion of Palantir stock on his social media platform shortly after acquiring a significant amount himself, even providing the stock ticker abbreviation to followers. Maddow described these actions as 'fantastically kaleidoscopically corrupt,' suggesting that they could constitute conflicts of interest and market manipulation. The report emphasizes the overlap between Trump's

item.title: Cyprus Environment Commissioner Urges Stop to Akamas Road Expansion Over Ecological Concerns
item.description: Environment Commissioner Antonia Theodosiou has formally requested that the Baths of Aphrodite-Fontana Amorosa roadwork project in Akamas be immediately halted and that its environmental approval be withdrawn. She cites violations of legal and ecological regulations, warning that the ongoing works risk irreversible damage to protected coastal and forest ecosystems. The commissioner highlights that the approved road design exceeds previous ecological assessment limits, with widening operations surpassing the previously allowed 300-meter threshold. She also notes that coastal protection requirements were inadequately studied, potentially placing Cyprus at risk of breaching the Barcelona Protocol. Governance issues were raised due to the forestry department acting both as project promoter and as part of the ecological assessment committee, creating potential conflicts of interest. Environmental groups, including Friends of Akamas, have echoed calls to revoke approval, arguing the project

item.title: Trump asked Thune to remove Senate parliamentarian after ruling against ballroom funding, but Thune refused
item.description: According to reports, former President Donald Trump privately requested that Senate Majority Leader John Thune remove Senate Parliamentarian Elizabeth MacDonough after she ruled that Republicans could not include funding for Trump's private ballroom in a budget bill. The bill, primarily focused on funding Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and Border Patrol, initially included $1 billion for security-related features of the ballroom, which Trump had previously claimed would be covered entirely by donors. Thune, however, firmly declined to dismiss MacDonough, noting that the parliamentarian's role has historically frustrated majorities from both parties and that the process for reconciliation bills must be followed. While the parliamentarian's decisions are technically advisory and can be overridden by a simple majority, doing so would effectively invoke the 'nuclear option' against the Senate's 60-vote filibuster rule, which most Republicans are hesitant to use due to concerns a

item.title: Photographer Jozef Macak Documents Life Along Dhaka’s Polluted Buriganga River
item.description: Slovak photographer Jozef Macak presents a compelling visual study of life along the Buriganga River in Dhaka, Bangladesh, through his project 'Tides of Life.' Known for its heavy pollution and nearing 'biological death,' the Buriganga remains a central part of daily life for millions of people who rely on it for transport, work, and recreation. Macak, a self-taught photographer, shifted from traditional travel and landscape photography to street photography workshops, where he developed a more personal and observational approach. His work captures the river's bustling activity—from overloaded boats and improvised bridges to workers, boat traffic, and everyday routines—highlighting the delicate balance between human resilience and environmental degradation. While the river struggles to sustain life due to industrial waste and untreated sewage, it continues to serve as a lifeline for the surrounding community. Macak’s images emphasize movement, energy, and the ingenuity of those navigat

item.title: Analysis Highlights Inconsistencies in Comey Indictment Compared to Other Threat Cases
item.description: A national security journalist, Marcy Wheeler of Emptywheel, has identified notable inconsistencies in the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey, suggesting political motivations behind the case. Wheeler compared Comey's indictment with four other threat cases prosecuted by Trump-appointed U.S. Attorney W. Ellis Boyle in the Eastern District of North Carolina. Unlike the other cases, Comey's indictment was announced immediately in a high-profile press conference featuring Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche and FBI Director Kash Patel. Other defendants, including Christopher James Hill, were quietly announced weeks after their arrests. Wheeler also highlighted that Comey is the only defendant facing a forfeiture allegation, and his alleged threat was described in far more detail than others. Legal experts have criticized the so-called '8647' seashell post at the center of Comey’s case as constitutionally questionable. The case reportedly only gained momentum after Trump dismis

item.title: Jim Cramer Comments on Trump's Extensive Stock Trading Amid Ethical Concerns
item.description: During a CNBC segment, financial analyst Jim Cramer appeared visibly confused while discussing President Donald Trump's recent stock trading activities. Government Ethics Office disclosures revealed that Trump executed over 3,700 stock trades in the first quarter of 2026, including more than 30 trades exceeding $1 million each. His transactions involved major companies such as Tesla, Nvidia, Apple, Meta, and Boeing—many of whose executives recently traveled with Trump to China. Journalist Judd Legum highlighted potential insider trading patterns on his Substack, noting that Trump purchased shares in companies he publicly praised or visited, including Thermo Fisher, Apple, and Micron. Trump also encouraged public purchases, such as recommending Dell computers shortly after buying Dell shares. Representative Dan Goldman described the activity as 'blatant and criminal insider trading,' emphasizing the need for careful record-keeping for future investigations. The segment left viewers with

item.title: FBI Seeks to Purchase Nationwide License Plate Data for Law Enforcement and Intelligence Purposes
item.description: The FBI has announced plans to spend up to $36 million to acquire nationwide access to automated license plate reader (ALPR) data. The agency aims to obtain detailed vehicle information—including license plate numbers, make, model, color, timestamps, and location data—from across the United States, its territories, and other areas such as Guam, Puerto Rico, and Tribal territories. This data would allow the FBI to track vehicle movements over time and query the information through a Software-as-a-Service system. The initiative is part of the FBI's Directorate of Intelligence mission, which supports both law enforcement and intelligence activities. The agency intends to award the contract to a single vendor but may split it between two vendors if necessary. Companies such as Flock and Motorola Solutions are among the potential providers capable of supplying this level of data. The move has prompted public discussion about privacy, government surveillance, and the potential misuse of auto

item.title: Anderson Cooper Discusses Pardoned January 6 Rioter Linked to Fraudulent Restitution Claim
item.description: CNN's Anderson Cooper highlighted a surprising case involving a pardoned participant in the January 6 Capitol insurrection. Edward Kelly, convicted in 2024 for plotting to kill FBI agents who investigated him, is serving a life sentence. In contrast, Andrew Paul Johnson, another rioter, received a pardon from former President Trump last year but was later arrested in Florida on child sex abuse charges. Johnson allegedly attempted to pay one of his victims $10 million, calling it 'restitution' for his involvement in January 6. Cooper noted the unusual timing, as the Trump Department of Justice had established a $1.776 billion fund to compensate individuals claiming they were wrongly prosecuted by the Biden administration. Leaked guidelines specify that the government cannot be held liable for crimes committed after the funds are disbursed, prompting Cooper to question whether Johnson might actually qualify for the payment. The case illustrates the complexities and controversies surround

item.title: Canada Confirms One Hantavirus Case from Cruise Ship Outbreak
item.description: Canada's Public Health Agency confirmed that one Canadian who recently returned from a cruise ship affected by a hantavirus outbreak has tested positive for the virus. The case involves a person in their 70s from Yukon, who was hospitalized in Victoria along with a traveling partner, who tested negative. Four Canadian passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius returned to British Columbia last Sunday, all of whom are currently in isolation. The outbreak on the ship has resulted in three deaths, including a Dutch couple who are believed to have contracted the virus in South America. To date, ten people from the ship, including the Canadian patient, have tested positive for hantavirus. Health officials emphasize that the overall risk to the general Canadian population remains low, as all confirmed cases are limited to passengers or crew from the cruise ship. Canadian authorities are monitoring the situation and taking precautionary measures to protect citizens while continuing investigat

item.title: Modern Spacecraft Materials Increase Risk of Surviving Debris Reentering Earth's Atmosphere
item.description: The increasing use of heat-resistant materials in spacecraft is changing the way space debris behaves during reentry, raising safety concerns worldwide. Traditionally, satellites and rocket components would disintegrate completely when reentering Earth's atmosphere, posing minimal risk. However, modern spacecraft use carbon fiber-reinforced plastics and advanced alloys that survive extreme heat, allowing larger fragments to reach the surface. Incidents include debris from SpaceX's Dragon capsules and carbon fiber components carrying pressurized gases, found in North America, Australia, and Europe. Researchers are studying ways to modify these materials so they can safely disintegrate without compromising spacecraft performance. The physics behind reentry involves objects traveling at over 17,000 miles per hour colliding with atmospheric molecules, generating heat above 3,000°F. While traditional materials melt, advanced composites can endure longer, making debris less predictable. The

item.title: Curated Highlights of the Best Photobooks of 2025
item.description: LensCulture has compiled a comprehensive list of over 50 photobooks that stood out in 2025, curated by photography experts and enthusiasts worldwide. The collection spans a remarkable diversity of styles, formats, and cultural perspectives, including self-published zines, retrospectives, innovative multi-book sets, and long-term projects capturing daily life, protest movements, or the intricacies of human relationships. Featured photographers range from Angela Christofilou documenting London protests with empathy and depth, to Coreen Simpson, whose portraits and diverse visual work are elegantly showcased. Other notable works include Judith Nangala Crispin’s fusion of Aboriginal traditions and printmaking, Kazunari Suzuki’s analysis of Japan’s visual clichés, and Co Rentmeester’s documentation of historical events redefining visual memory. Additional highlights explore themes of memory, identity, intimacy, and the intersection of culture and place, such as A Yin’s portrayal of the Bury

item.title: Megyn Kelly criticizes Trump administration over exaggerated Cuba threat report
item.description: Megyn Kelly strongly criticized the Trump administration during her Monday podcast over a recent Axios report claiming Cuba had acquired over 300 military drones and might target U.S. military sites such as Guantanamo Bay, naval vessels, and Key West. Kelly dismissed the report as exaggerated and misleading, highlighting Cuba's current economic and infrastructure struggles. She argued that the article seemed designed to manufacture a pretext for military action, noting that even Axios admitted the intelligence involved contingency planning rather than an imminent threat. Kelly questioned the timing of the report, pointing to recent visits by CIA Director John Ratcliffe to Havana, potential DOJ actions against Raul Castro, and political statements from figures like Lindsey Graham. Journalist Ryan Grim, who recently visited Cuba, confirmed that Cuban officials indicated willingness to negotiate with the U.S., making a military buildup unnecessary according to Kelly. She framed the report

item.title: A Visual Journey Through the 1939 New York World’s Fair
item.description: The 1939 New York World’s Fair, held in Flushing Meadows, Queens, remains one of the most ambitious events of the 20th century. Celebrating the theme 'The World of Tomorrow,' it showcased futuristic architecture, technological innovations, and cultural exhibitions that reflected both optimism and curiosity on the brink of World War II. Iconic landmarks such as the Trylon and Perisphere became symbols of modernist design, while the fairgrounds featured numerous pavilions, performances, and attractions capturing the imagination of millions of visitors. From the Westinghouse Moto-Man Elektro to precision swimming at Billy Rose's Aquacade, the fair offered entertainment, education, and spectacle. Visitors witnessed technological marvels like the Futurama exhibit, interacted with displays demonstrating advances in plastics and household appliances, and explored international pavilions highlighting global culture. The fair also included playful attractions such as parachute rides, roller ska

item.title: Airline Industry Faces Potential Struggles Due to Iran War and Fuel Shortages
item.description: Airline industry executives are preparing for potential crises similar to the Spirit Airlines liquidation earlier this year, as geopolitical tensions, particularly the ongoing Iran war, threaten the global supply of jet fuel. The conflict has disrupted the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping lane responsible for 20% of the world's oil transit, causing widespread fuel shortages and price increases. European budget carriers like Ryanair are monitoring the situation closely. Ryanair's CFO, Neil Sorahan, confirmed that while they are currently maintaining full flight schedules, weaker airlines may struggle and potentially go out of business during the winter months. The Trump administration previously attempted to bail out Spirit Airlines, proposing a government acquisition of up to 90% of the company, but creditor objections prevented the deal. With no clear resolution to the Iran war in sight, airline executives are taking precautions against further disruptions that could destabilize the

item.title: Pentagon spokesperson defends escort policy as reporters dispute First Amendment concerns
item.description: Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell responded sharply to the New York Times' second lawsuit against the Department of Defense in five months, arguing that the paper seeks unrestricted access to classified information. The lawsuit challenges the policy requiring journalists to be escorted at all times while inside the Pentagon. Parnell claimed that allowing reporters to roam freely would grant them privileges unavailable in other federal buildings. Reporters immediately pushed back, noting that no evidence exists showing that journalists obtained classified material while walking the Pentagon halls, which are largely unclassified and visited by civilians and foreign personnel. Reporters also highlighted that employees in common areas like the food court move unescorted, and that previous press corps access for decades did not lead to security breaches. Commentators and military veterans criticized Parnell's stance as an exaggeration of security risks, arguing that the policy primarily restr

item.title: Philadelphia Eagles Wide Receiver A.J. Brown Marries Kelsey Riley at Luxury Laguna Beach Ceremony
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown celebrated his wedding to Kelsey Riley on May 16, 2026, at the Montage Laguna Beach resort in California. The ceremony, which took place at 5 PM overlooking the ocean, included elegant touches such as a violinist greeting guests outside the reception area. Brown's son had a memorable moment entering the celebration to applause, carrying a couple of Skittles. Guests were asked to follow an earthy-toned dress code, specifically wearing brown. Among the attendees was fellow Eagles player Darius Slay serving as a groomsman. The reception offered gourmet treats, including mini lobster rolls and mini tacos, and the celebration continued well past 9 PM. Security was tight, ensuring privacy for the high-profile event. Brown had proposed to Riley in May of the previous year at the Four Seasons in Philadelphia, arranging a private performance by John Legend and celebrating the moment with his teammates. The wedding reflects both the couple’s taste for

item.title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth campaigns for Trump-backed candidate in Kentucky primary
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth temporarily set aside his responsibilities related to the war in Iran to campaign in Kentucky's 4th Congressional District primary. He attended an event in Hebron supporting Ed Gallrein, the candidate favored by former President Donald Trump, who is challenging incumbent Rep. Thomas Massie, a Republican known for criticizing Trump. Massie has publicly questioned Trump's associations with Jeffrey Epstein and challenged the party's core values, prompting Trump to endorse opponents against his critics in multiple states. Hegseth’s participation marks an unusual level of political involvement for a sitting Pentagon chief during active military operations, raising questions among political observers about the appropriateness of such engagement. This direct involvement reflects a broader pattern of Trump backing candidates against Republicans who opposed him on key issues, including impeachment and redistricting matters. Gallrein’s campaign in Kentucky is there

item.title: The Art of Freezing Anticipation: Kyle Bean and Aaron Tilley’s Series
item.description: The article highlights the striking visual work by designer Kyle Bean and photographer Aaron Tilley, who collaborate in a series named 'Anxious Anticipation.' The series captures moments of high tension, just before everyday objects cause chaotic interactions. Through meticulously designed sets, the artists freeze time to show objects in mid-motion, including smashing champagne glasses, cracking eggs, and drops of ink falling on a crisp white shirt. The photographs convey a sense of potential energy, capturing the suspense and beauty in what might otherwise seem like ordinary actions. The article emphasizes the emotional impact of these freeze-frame moments, revealing the artistry behind the composition and the anticipation before movement and chaos unfold. Moss & Fog shares these images with the artists’ permission, showcasing the intersection of design, photography, and conceptual art. The post also contextualizes the series within the broader content of the site, which celebrates vi

item.title: Well-Preserved 19th-Century Daguerreotype Recovered from SS Central America Wreck
item.description: In 2014, explorers recovered a remarkably well-preserved daguerreotype of an unidentified woman from the wreck of the SS Central America, a ship that sank in 1857 off the coast of South Carolina during a Category 2 hurricane. The ship, which carried over 30,000 pounds of gold from California miners, was part of a critical financial transfer during the Panic of 1857. While the majority of photographs found in the wreck were degraded, this particular daguerreotype, nicknamed the 'Mona Lisa of the Deep' for the woman's enigmatic smile, remained intact due to the cold, dark, and oxygen-poor conditions of the deep ocean and the metal-based composition of daguerreotypes. Historians suggest she may have been a relative of one of the miners, highlighting a personal connection amid the lost riches. The portrait sold at auction in March 2023 for $73,800. The wreck of the SS Central America was first located in 1988 at a depth of 7,200 feet, making the recovery of such delicate artifacts a signif

item.title: Critics Question DOJ $1.776 Billion Compensation Fund Linked to Trump Allies
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) has established a $1.776 billion taxpayer-funded compensation fund aimed at President Donald Trump's allies, including individuals involved in the January 6 Capitol events, who claim political targeting by previous administrations. The fund was created as part of a settlement in which Trump, his sons Donald Jr. and Eric, and the Trump Organization agreed to drop a $10 billion IRS lawsuit. This arrangement comes amid judicial concerns about the legitimacy of the case given Trump's influence over the DOJ. Critics have described the fund as a potential 'slush fund' and raised alarms over the lack of transparency and oversight. Legal experts argue that taxpayers’ money could be misused, with funds disproportionately benefiting Trump's wealthy associates rather than participants in the January 6 events. Reports indicate that if Democrats regain control of one or both chambers of Congress, they could potentially shut down the fund and require disclosure o

item.title: European Military Buildup and the Risks of Escalating Tensions with Russia
item.description: The article discusses current geopolitical tensions in Europe, focusing on Germany's plans to strengthen its military. Berlin aims to transform the Bundeswehr into the strongest conventional army in Europe by 2035 and a technologically superior force by 2039, marking exactly a century since Germany initiated World War II in Europe. The author warns against rushing into nuclear confrontation, criticizing both NATO advocates and Russian hardliners for their aggressive postures. He emphasizes that reported Ukrainian advances are tactical and do not indicate the collapse of Russia, which has been repeatedly predicted over the past four years of conflict. Additionally, the author raises concerns about the recruitment of immigrants or their children into the German army, questioning their loyalty and potential susceptibility to alternative ideological influences. The article also critiques European media, arguing that the so-called 'free press' often presents a biased, anti-Russian narrative

item.title: Stolen $24,000 Pinot Noir Returned to Virginia Restaurant Before Court Hearing
item.description: In Virginia, a $24,000 bottle of Pinot Noir that had been stolen from a high-end French restaurant was recovered just before a scheduled court hearing. Sgt. Mike Bell delivered the bottle to the restaurant's kitchen, surprising the owners with its unexpected return. The theft had drawn considerable attention due to the high value of the wine, highlighting both the audacity of the heist and the unusual circumstances surrounding its recovery. After the court proceedings, a defense attorney described the case as resembling 'an episode of Scooby-Doo,' reflecting the bizarre and almost cinematic nature of the incident. The wine's return closes a chapter on a case that had captured local interest, raising questions about restaurant security and the motivations behind such high-value thefts. The incident has been widely reported due to the combination of the luxury item involved and the almost storybook resolution of the crime.

item.title: Remembering Suzanne Pleshette: Iconic Actress of Stage and Screen
item.description: Suzanne Pleshette (1937–2008) was a celebrated American actress known for her distinctive husky voice and sharp comedic timing. She began her career in serious drama on Broadway and in films such as Alfred Hitchcock’s 'The Birds,' but she gained her greatest fame in the 1970s as Emily Hartley on 'The Bob Newhart Show.' In this role, Pleshette transformed the image of the sitcom wife, portraying a character who was intelligent, independent, and an equal partner to her husband, all delivered with her signature dry humor. Her versatility allowed her to move effortlessly between high-stakes drama and sophisticated comedy over a career spanning five decades, earning multiple Emmy and Golden Globe nominations. Off-screen, Pleshette was admired for her down-to-earth personality and candid approach to life, which contributed to her lasting popularity. Her legacy remains as one of the most charismatic and enduring figures from television’s Golden Era, remembered not only for her performances bu

item.title: Analysis Finds Majority of College Commencement Speakers Lean Democratic
item.description: A recent analysis by The College Fix has revealed that at top-ranked U.S. universities, Democratic or Democratic-leaning speakers significantly outnumber Republican speakers at commencement ceremonies. The study examined public statements and donation records to identify political affiliations among speakers at main or undergraduate ceremonies. Out of the total partisan speakers identified, 86% were Democrats. Notable Republican speakers included Arthur Brooks at Vanderbilt University, Dario Gil at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, and former NFL player Colt McCoy at the University of Texas at Austin. In contrast, Democratic speakers included governors such as Gretchen Whitmer at Michigan State University and Abigail Spanberger at Virginia Tech, entertainers like Sarah Jessica Parker at Northwestern University, and authors including James Patterson at the University of Wisconsin-Madison. The report highlights that conservative speakers on campuses often face hostility, with some cancel

item.title: OSHA Opens Investigation into Worker Death at SpaceX Starbase Launch Site in Texas
item.description: A worker died at SpaceX’s Starbase facility in South Texas early on May 15, 2026, prompting the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) to launch a formal investigation. The incident occurred around 4:17 a.m. local time at the company’s active launch and construction site. Details surrounding the circumstances of the death remain limited as OSHA has stated it will not release additional information until the probe concludes, a process that could take several months. Local authorities, including the county sheriff, confirmed the fatality, while SpaceX and the City of Starbase have not commented. This event comes at a critical time for SpaceX, just days before the planned first launch of its upgraded Starship rocket and ahead of the anticipated release of the prospectus for what is expected to be one of the largest initial public offerings in history. SpaceX has faced ongoing scrutiny regarding worker safety at Starbase. Previous analyses of OSHA data showed the site had sig

item.title: CBO Estimates $1.2 Trillion Cost for Golden Dome Missile Defense Program
item.description: A new Congressional Budget Office (CBO) assessment estimates that the Golden Dome missile-defense system could cost $1.2 trillion to fully implement, significantly exceeding the Trump administration’s initial projections. The administration had promised a cost of $175 billion, while its current five-year budget allocates only $79 billion. The system, which includes space-based interceptors, would not provide an impenetrable defense against large-scale attacks, particularly from peer adversaries. Roughly $730 billion of the projected cost would purchase enough space-based interceptors to destroy about ten incoming ballistic missiles. Analysts argue that the administration’s current funding levels would only provide incremental improvements to the existing missile defense system. The Pentagon has relied heavily on reconciliation funds outside the baseline defense budget to support Golden Dome, with $24 billion obtained last year and $17 billion requested for 2027. The CBO noted that omit

item.title: Florida Congressman Criticizes Kentucky Republican Massie Amid GOP Primary Tensions
item.description: Florida Congressman Randy Fine appeared on Real America's Voice to publicly criticize fellow Republican Thomas Massie of Kentucky, highlighting a growing rift within the GOP ahead of the primary election. Fine accused Massie of associating with extremist figures, including a podcaster wearing Nazi symbols, and amplified allegations from Massie's former girlfriend claiming misuse of congressional influence and inappropriate behavior. Additionally, Fine suggested Massie had connections with Iranian-linked interests, which Massie denies, explaining financial assistance was for her relocation and unrelated to employment or political influence. Fine framed these claims in the context of loyalty to former President Donald Trump, echoing Trump's efforts to unseat Massie, whom Trump has repeatedly criticized and labeled one of the worst Republican congressmen. Trump personally recruited Massie's primary challenger, former Navy SEAL Ed Gallrein, and threatened consequences for GOP members who s

item.title: Intimate 1969 Portraits of Johnny Cash by Michael Rougier
item.description: In 1969, renowned photographer Michael Rougier captured a series of intimate and revealing photographs of Johnny Cash for LIFE magazine. Unlike the gritty, performance-focused images from Cash's famous Folsom and San Quentin prison concerts, Rougier's work highlighted a transformative period in Cash's life. This year marked a personal and professional rebirth: Cash had recently married June Carter, celebrated the success of his live prison albums, and began filming The Johnny Cash Show at the Ryman Auditorium. Among the notable photographs are Cash walking along railway tracks near his Tennessee home with his guitar, and portraits in front of Nashville's historic Dixie 576 steam locomotive. Rougier also documented recording sessions at Columbia Studio B and captured candid moments with family, friends, and fellow artists like Glen Campbell. Known for his 'fly-on-the-wall' approach, Rougier was able to portray Cash authentically, offering viewers a rare glimpse into the life of a man na

item.title: Bo Bichette Discusses Performance Struggles and Future with the Mets
item.description: Bo Bichette, who signed a three-year, $126 million deal with the New York Mets, has faced a challenging start to the 2026 season. The Mets, currently 20-26 and struggling in the NL East, have raised questions about Bichette's role in the team’s long-term plans, especially given his two opt-outs after the 2026 and 2027 seasons. Despite this, Bichette stated that he is focused on helping the team first and is not considering his opt-out options at this time. Offensively, Bichette has struggled significantly, batting .210/.262/.269 with only seven extra-base hits, and has not had an extra-base hit in his last 61 at-bats. His .531 OPS is among the worst in the league. In addition to adjusting to a new position at third base, Bichette has briefly returned to shortstop following an injury to Francisco Lindor, which may have contributed to his offensive slump. Defensively, he has committed only two errors in 33 appearances at third base. Despite recent challenges, including a defensive mispla

item.title: London Tube Strikes Suspended After Last-Minute Agreement Between Union and TfL
item.description: London Underground strikes that were scheduled for this week have been suspended following a last-minute shift in position by Transport for London (TfL). The Rail, Maritime and Transport (RMT) union had planned 24-hour walkouts on Tuesday and Thursday over concerns regarding new rosters, fatigue, and safety issues related to a proposed four-day working week. The union announced the suspension on Monday after TfL changed its stance, allowing further discussions on members' concerns. However, the dispute is far from resolved, with the RMT warning that additional strike action could follow if progress is not made. Strikes previously planned for mid-June have also been called off, but new dates of June 2 and June 4 have been set as potential action days. TfL's director of customer operations expressed pleasure at the decision, highlighting the benefits of the voluntary four-day week for both drivers' work-life balance and service reliability. Business representatives welcomed the news but

item.title: Security Guard Killed in Shooting at San Diego Mosque; Two Suspects Die by Apparent Suicide
item.description: A security guard was killed in a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego, the largest mosque in San Diego County, on Monday, May 18, 2026. According to law enforcement officials, two men believed to be the suspects were found dead in a car nearby from apparent self-inflicted gunshot wounds. Police stated that the threat has been neutralized and there is no ongoing danger to the community. The incident occurred in the Clairemont neighborhood, prompting a swift response from local police and firefighters. All children, teachers, and staff at the mosque and affiliated school were reported safe, though nearby schools including Bright Horizons Academy and Kavod Charter were placed on lockdown as a precaution. Witnesses described hearing multiple rounds of gunfire, possibly from a semiautomatic weapon, and seeing the security guard shot before he stood back up. Parents were reunited with children at a nearby reunification center. The FBI is assisting local authorities with the investigat

item.title: Larry Hogan Criticizes GOP Alignment with Trump and Promotes Ethical Leadership
item.description: Larry Hogan, former Republican governor of Maryland and current U.S. Senate hopeful, has expressed growing disillusionment with the Republican Party as it increasingly aligns with former President Donald Trump. Hogan, known for his bipartisan approval ratings during his governorship, acknowledges that his attempts to leverage this record for a national political role were hindered by Trump's influence over the party. Describing himself as entering a 'Zen era,' Hogan has stepped away from party politics and now leads the Hogan Institute at a liberal arts college, where he focuses on teaching ethical political leadership. Hogan warned that Republicans are too often hesitant to criticize Trump due to fear of primary challenges or social media backlash, a trend he believes undermines political courage. His remarks come as Trump seeks to tighten his grip on the party during the 2026 midterms, endorsing candidates who oppose lawmakers challenging his policies. Hogan hopes that electoral loss

item.title: Kimchi-Derived Probiotic Shows Potential to Reduce Nanoplastic Accumulation in the Body
item.description: Researchers in South Korea have identified a strain of probiotic bacteria from kimchi, Leuconostoc mesenteroides CBA3656, that may help the body remove harmful nanoplastics. Nanoplastics, particles smaller than one micrometer, can enter the human body through food and water, potentially accumulating in organs such as the kidneys and brain. Laboratory tests showed that this kimchi-derived bacterium strongly binds to polystyrene nanoplastics, maintaining a 57% adsorption rate under conditions mimicking the human intestine, whereas a reference strain dropped to just 3%. Experiments in germ-free mice revealed that those receiving the probiotic excreted more than double the amount of nanoplastics compared to untreated mice. These results suggest that certain microbes in fermented foods may offer a biological method to mitigate the health risks posed by plastic pollution. The study, published in Bioresource Technology, highlights the potential of traditional fermented foods not only in suppo

item.title: Minnesota ICE Agent Faces Felony Charges for Shooting Venezuelan Immigrant
item.description: Minnesota prosecutors have issued a warrant for the arrest of a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agent who allegedly shot a Venezuelan immigrant during a federal enforcement operation in Minneapolis earlier this year. The incident occurred as part of a broader crackdown, during which three individuals were reportedly shot. The agent now faces felony assault charges. Prosecutors have stated that this action is part of a thorough investigation into the use of force during federal immigration operations. The case has drawn attention to ICE enforcement methods and raised questions about accountability, particularly in large-scale operations targeting immigrant communities. Officials are expected to pursue legal proceedings to ensure the agent faces appropriate consequences if found guilty. The case also highlights the broader tensions between immigration enforcement and civil rights protections, underscoring the ongoing debate about the balance between public safety and human

item.title: Former Lawmaker Criticizes Trump's Handling of Iran Conflict and Its Impact on U.S. Strategy
item.description: Former Representative Adam Kinzinger, a Republican from Illinois and outspoken critic of former President Donald Trump, criticized Trump's handling of the ongoing military situation with Iran. Following Trump's announcement to halt a planned military attack in favor of 'serious negotiations' while keeping forces on alert, Kinzinger argued that the president's statements have left the United States in a vulnerable position. He expressed concern over Trump's pattern of issuing threats and then backing down, which he believes undermines U.S. credibility and strategy. Kinzinger highlighted the lack of a clear plan to manage or resolve the conflict and warned that current actions could lead to prolonged uncertainty, affecting international trade and future generations. He also emphasized the need for concrete contingencies if military action is to be pursued, stressing that without them, the country risks worsening its strategic position. Kinzinger's comments underscore a broader debate abo

item.title: Historical Ambrotype Portraits of Japanese Individuals During the Meiji Era
item.description: In the late 19th century, Japan experienced significant social and cultural transformation during the Meiji Era, a period when the country rapidly modernized. During this time, ambrotypes, locally known as garasu-shashin (glass photos), became a favored medium for capturing personal and family portraits. These images, created using underexposed glass negatives backed with black material to produce a positive image, offered striking clarity and a hauntingly vivid quality. Often preserved in intricately carved Kiri-wood (Paulownia) cases, ambrotypes were valued as more than simple photographs; they were considered sacred family keepsakes. The portraits showcased a blend of traditional Japanese attire, such as kimonos and hakama, alongside emerging Western influences, like bowler hats. Subjects ranged from children and young adults to elderly figures, often depicted with a stoic grace that reflected the societal shifts of the era. Some ambrotypes were uniquely bound in paper or featured u

item.title: Indonesia Receives New Rafale Jets and Modernizes Military Equipment
item.description: On May 18, 2026, Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto announced the continued modernization of the nation's defense capabilities during a handover ceremony at a military airbase in Jakarta. The ceremony featured the delivery of six French-made Rafale fighter jets, part of a larger $8.1 billion deal signed in 2022 for 42 aircraft. In addition, the Indonesian military received four Dassault Falcon 8X and one Airbus A400M Atlas aircraft. President Prabowo emphasized that strengthening defense capabilities is essential for national security and stability in an unpredictable global geopolitical environment. Defence Ministry spokesman Rico Ricardo Sirait highlighted that modernizing military equipment is not only about acquiring combat platforms but also about safeguarding Indonesia's sovereignty, national honor, and defense readiness. Prabowo, who assumed office in 2024, has prioritized modernizing Indonesia's aging military assets and has sought international cooperation, including discus

item.title: Rev. Franklin Graham Criticized for Speech at Trump's National Prayer Event
item.description: At the Rededicate 250 event held at the National Mall in Washington, D.C., Rev. Franklin Graham delivered a pre-recorded video message expressing strong criticism of America's moral state. He described the nation as 'morally rotten' and 'completely sick with sin,' citing transgender rights and the allowance of men in women's locker rooms as examples of societal decline. Graham also compared current violence to the biblical story of the flood, highlighting rapes, murders, and violent content in video games. His statements have drawn widespread criticism for selectively targeting certain moral issues while ignoring broader societal problems such as corruption, wars, wealth inequality, and sexual abuse by clergy. Political commentators on social media highlighted the omission of these larger systemic issues, labeling Graham's speech as overly focused on bigotry and fear-mongering rather than comprehensive moral critique. The event and its messaging have reignited debates over the intersec

item.title: A Look at Betty Hutton’s Career and Iconic Portraits from the 1940s
item.description: Betty Hutton, born Elizabeth June Thornburg on February 26, 1921, was a multi-talented American performer known for her work as an actress, singer, dancer, and comedian. She became a major star in the 1940s after signing with Paramount Pictures, where she primarily appeared in musicals. Hutton’s energetic and vibrant style quickly gained attention, earning her a reputation as one of the studio’s most valuable talents. She began the decade on Broadway with notable productions like 'Two for the Show' and 'Panama Hattie', where her high-energy performances were so intense that some songs had to be removed to balance the shows. Hutton’s film debut came in 1942 with 'The Fleet’s In', and she achieved critical acclaim with her role in Preston Sturges’s comedy 'The Miracle of Morgan’s Creek' in 1944, demonstrating her versatility in both comedy and drama. During World War II, she was a favorite among troops, starring in morale-boosting films such as 'Star Spangled Rhythm' and 'Here Come the W

item.title: Exploring Everyday Life in Switzerland Through Pelin Guven’s Street Photography
item.description: Turkish photographer Pelin Guven offers a fresh perspective on Swiss life through her street photography series, 'Absence.' Moving from Asia to Switzerland changed her approach to capturing the world, as the country’s quiet, reserved streets contrasted sharply with the bustling cities she had previously experienced. Guven focuses on small gestures, fleeting expressions, and subtle bursts of color, revealing moments often overlooked in daily life. Her background in psychology informs her sensitivity to human behavior, light, and shadow, helping her capture scenes that evoke tension, humor, or mystery. Through her images, she highlights the contrast between Switzerland’s order and the creativity that emerges underneath, echoing the playful and innovative works of Swiss artists like Fischli/Weiss and Pipilotti Rist. Bold use of shadow and negative space draws attention to details that might otherwise go unnoticed—a woman's eyes behind glasses, a slice of glowing watermelon, or a shock of

item.title: Papa Johns Begins Drone Delivery Tests in North Carolina Using Sandwiches
item.description: Papa Johns has partnered with Wing, Alphabet's drone delivery company, to launch a test program in Indian Trail, North Carolina, near Charlotte. Instead of starting with its signature pizzas, the initial deliveries feature a limited menu of oven-toasted sandwiches such as Philly Cheesesteak, Chicken Bacon Ranch, and Steak & Mushroom. Customers in the eligible area can order through the Wing app. The decision to begin with sandwiches stems from practical challenges with pizza boxes, which are large, flat, and difficult to keep stable during drone flight without risking toppings sliding or cheese melting unevenly. Drones have payload limits and require compact, aerodynamic packaging for safe and efficient transport. Papa Johns and Wing are collaborating on custom packaging solutions that could eventually support pizza deliveries. The test represents Wing's first direct partnership with a national restaurant brand and builds on Papa Johns' existing use of Google Cloud technology. Long-ter

item.title: A Look Back at ABC Film Review Magazine Covers from the 1950s
item.description: ABC Film Review, first published in 1950, quickly became a major part of British cinema culture. Originally intended as a promotional magazine for the Associated British Cinemas (ABC) circuit, it evolved into the UK’s longest-running film publication, offering readers more than just marketing content. The magazine featured glossy portraits of celebrities, behind-the-scenes stories, and detailed film reviews, which influenced public opinion and cinematic tastes for decades. Its coverage spanned the transition from the Golden Age of Hollywood to the emerging blockbuster era, making it a valuable historical record of film trends and celebrity culture. Although digital media eventually replaced print magazines, ABC Film Review’s legacy continues to evoke nostalgia among cinephiles and collectors. The article showcases a collection of ABC Film Review covers from the 1950s, highlighting the visual and cultural significance of these publications for generations of movie enthusiasts.

item.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Briefly Delayed by Chinese Security During State Visit
item.description: During a recent trip to China, U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent experienced a brief but tense encounter with Chinese security officials outside the Great Hall of the People in Beijing. The incident occurred ahead of a state banquet held in honor of President Donald Trump. Footage circulating online shows Bessent being stopped at the entrance, reportedly due to not wearing the required clearance pin. After a short discussion with security and receiving the correct item from his aides, Bessent was allowed to enter the building. White House reporters accompanying the U.S. delegation described the delay as an 'intense' 30-minute standoff, with security personnel from both countries refusing to yield initially. This incident highlights heightened scrutiny and differing accounts between the White House and Beijing regarding the summit. Observers also noted concerns about Trump’s approach to Taiwan and potential implications for U.S.-China relations, as the president has shown reluctance

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Portraits of Elizabeth Taylor on the Set of Cleopatra, 1962
item.description: In 1962, French photographer Philippe Le Tellier captured a series of striking behind-the-scenes portraits of Elizabeth Taylor during the filming of Cleopatra at Rome's Cinecittà studios. These photographs stand out not only for showcasing Taylor in her elaborate royal costumes but also for revealing her natural, relaxed demeanor amidst one of the most famously extravagant and stressful film productions in Hollywood history. Taken primarily in March 1962, Le Tellier's work features Taylor in iconic outfits, including her signature blue crown and golden headdress, highlighting the meticulous Academy Award-winning costume design. Beyond their visual appeal, these portraits provide a rare glimpse into the life of a leading actress at the peak of her fame, portraying both the glamour and the human side of Taylor. Today, these images are celebrated as essential pieces of cinematic history, offering insight into the artistry, scale, and drama that defined Cleopatra's production and solidifyi

item.title: Antonia Bembo’s 1707 Opera ‘Ercole Amante’ Premieres in Paris Over Three Centuries After Its Composition
item.description: Antonia Bembo, born around 1640 in Venice, was a talented musician who fled an abusive marriage to settle in Paris in 1677. Though trained in music by prominent Venetian composers like Francesco Cavalli, Bembo’s personal life initially overshadowed her career. She left her husband and children behind to live safely in Paris, where she performed for Louis XIV and received royal support. Bembo composed numerous works, including cantatas, arias, and motets, eventually completing her opera “Ercole Amante” in 1707. Her manuscripts were handwritten and difficult to decipher, contributing to her obscurity. Despite this, her music survived in France’s National Library, though it remained largely unperformed for centuries. Scholarly research over decades, including the discovery of archival documents and the study of her compositions, allowed a deeper understanding of her life and work. The Paris Opera will stage “Ercole Amante” at Opéra Bastille on May 28, 2026, marking the first modern perfor

item.title: Brush Fire in Simi Valley Destroys Structure, Forces Evacuations
item.description: A rapidly spreading brush fire, named the Sandy Fire, broke out in Simi Valley, California, before 11 a.m. on Monday, May 18, 2026. Driven by dry conditions and light gusty winds, the fire quickly expanded and entered a residential neighborhood, destroying at least one building. Firefighters and emergency services issued evacuation orders for numerous homes across southern Simi Valley, extending warnings westward into northern Thousand Oaks. The blaze has burned approximately 183 acres as of Monday morning. Authorities are closely monitoring the situation, and additional updates are expected as firefighting efforts continue. Residents in the affected areas are urged to follow evacuation instructions and stay informed through local news channels. This incident highlights the persistent wildfire risk in Ventura County, particularly during periods of dry and windy weather, underscoring the importance of preparedness and rapid emergency response in wildfire-prone regions.

item.title: Ryanair urges suspension of EU passport control system amid long airport queues
item.description: Ryanair has strongly criticized the European Union and French authorities over the implementation of the EU Entry/Exit System (EES), which began on April 10. The airline claims the rollout has been poorly planned, resulting in hour-long passport control lines during the first May Bank Holiday weekend. EES requires non-EU citizens to provide fingerprints and facial images at border control, aiming to detect overstayers. However, Ryanair states that authorities were aware of the system’s activation for over three years but failed to ensure sufficient staffing, operational kiosks, or readiness at French airports. Neal McMahon, Ryanair’s Chief Operations Officer, highlighted that some passengers spent as long waiting at passport control as they did on flights themselves, with many missing their flights due to excessive queues. The airline has written to governments across the 29 countries implementing EES, urging a suspension of the system until September, after the peak summer travel seas

item.title: Joan Bennett's Early Career and Iconic Style in the 1920s
item.description: Joan Geraldine Bennett (1910–1990) was an American actress who began her career on stage before becoming a prominent film star. In the late 1920s, Bennett transitioned from a youthful stage ingénue to a leading lady in Hollywood. She made her professional Broadway debut at 18 in the play 'Jarnegan,' performing alongside her father, Richard Bennett. She quickly gained attention in films such as 'Bulldog Drummond' (1929) and 'Disraeli' (1929). Initially a natural blonde, Bennett often played sweet and youthful roles, contrasting with the darker 'femme fatale' image she later adopted in the 1940s. Her signature 1920s look included finger waves, soft curls, dropped-waist dresses, pearls, and cloche hats. Coming from the renowned Bennett acting family, Joan’s career benefited from her theatrical training, allowing her to transition seamlessly from silent films to talking pictures. Unlike her older sister Constance Bennett, known for sophisticated society roles, Joan was marketed as the char

item.title: First Impressions of Googlebook Laptops Raise Questions About Their Purpose
item.description: Google recently revealed its new laptop initiative, 'Googlebook,' during 'The Android Show,' which merges Android and ChromeOS with Gemini AI integrated features. The keynote highlighted a few new functionalities, most notably the 'Magic Pointer' that allows users to interact with content using simple gestures. Other features include 'Create your Widget,' Android app mirroring, and quick file access from Android devices, all of which either already exist on other platforms or are minor enhancements. The device also introduces 'premium hardware' and a signature 'Glow Bar.' Despite the hype, critics argue that Google has not demonstrated compelling reasons for the laptop’s existence, as smartphones already handle many AI-related tasks efficiently. While laptops are still essential for advanced computing tasks, Googlebook’s reliance on Android may limit access to professional-grade desktop apps like the Adobe suite. Overall, the announcement feels like a superficial attempt to create an A

item.title: Jury Rules Against Elon Musk in Lawsuit Over OpenAI Dispute
item.description: Elon Musk's legal battle against Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, OpenAI, and Microsoft has concluded with a unanimous verdict against him. Musk had accused the OpenAI co-founders and Microsoft of misappropriating charitable assets by establishing a for-profit branch of the AI research lab. The trial examined whether promises made to Musk were broken, but the jury ultimately ruled that Musk's claims were filed too late under the statute of limitations. Specific deadlines varied by claim, ranging from August 2021 to August 2022. Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers noted that the evidence strongly supported the jury’s decision, which made deliberations brief. As a result, the potential restructuring of OpenAI that Musk's lawsuit could have triggered is no longer a concern ahead of the company's reported IPO. Microsoft expressed support for the verdict, emphasizing its continued collaboration with OpenAI to advance AI globally. Musk’s legal team indicated plans to appeal the decision. This verdict hig

item.title: Trump-Backed Navy SEAL Candidate Ed Gallrein Accused of Inflating Bronze Star Count in Challenge to Rep. Thomas Massie
item.description: Ed Gallrein, a retired Navy SEAL and Republican congressional candidate endorsed by President Donald Trump to unseat Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky's 4th District, has responded angrily to a Daily Caller report questioning his military honors. The report highlighted inconsistencies in Gallrein's claimed awards, noting that while his recent campaign materials state he received four Bronze Stars, records and biographies dating back to 2011 consistently list him as having received three. These earlier references include official biographies, a 2016 Veterans Day event report, and other public mentions through 2024. Gallrein's campaign issued a statement denouncing the article as a 'lie-filled' smear orchestrated by Massie allies, but did not directly address or refute the discrepancy in the number of Bronze Stars. The primary has become the most expensive House primary in U.S. history, with over $25 million spent, much of it targeting Massie. Significant funding has come from pro-Israel gr

item.title: JD Vance recalls Trump criticizing his shamrock socks during St. Patrick’s Day visit
item.description: During a recent event in Kansas City, U.S. Vice President JD Vance shared a story highlighting former President Donald Trump's strict dress preferences. Vance recounted that last St. Patrick's Day, he wore shamrock-themed socks when welcoming Irish Prime Minister Micheál Martin at the Naval Observatory and then attending a meeting in the Oval Office. Trump reportedly noticed the socks immediately and asked, 'What is going on with those socks?' Vance used the incident to illustrate the importance of dressing conservatively around the President. The article also contextualizes Trump’s history of emphasizing attire, including giving Florsheim leather oxfords to Cabinet members and advising against sneakers. A previous notable incident occurred in February 2025, when Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy entered the Oval Office in his military-style attire, prompting Trump to comment on his clothing before later complimenting it. Overall, the story emphasizes Trump’s focus on formal dres

item.title: Cate Blanchett reflects on the decline of the #MeToo movement and evolving accountability in Hollywood
item.description: At the 2026 Cannes Film Festival, actress Cate Blanchett spoke candidly about the state of the #MeToo movement in Hollywood, expressing concern that its momentum was lost quickly. She highlighted the disparity between those with public platforms who can safely share experiences and the general public, questioning why wider voices are often silenced. Blanchett emphasized ongoing gender inequality in the film industry, noting that on many sets, men vastly outnumber women, which she believes contributes to repetitive and monotonous workplace culture. She also addressed the controversial issue of working with artists who have been accused of misconduct, suggesting that actors might consider collaborating with those who have shown genuine remorse and efforts to change. Drawing on examples like Woody Allen, she noted that public revelations about inappropriate behavior can be difficult to navigate, but she supports the idea that individuals can grow and evolve over time. Blanchett reinforced

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Missing Congressman While Office Continues Sending Newsletters
item.description: Rep. Tom Kean Jr., a New Jersey Republican, has been absent from Congress for 74 days, missing 68 votes and not appearing publicly. His office has attributed the absence to a 'personal health matter,' but details are scarce. Despite his absence, Kean's office has continued sending upbeat newsletters in his voice, which has raised concern among both constituents and fellow lawmakers. Aaron Fritschner, chief of staff to Rep. Don Beyer, and CNN journalist Andrew Kaczynski described the newsletters as unusual and unsettling given the congressman's prolonged absence. In response, Kean consultant Harrison Neely criticized Kaczynski for asking questions about Kean's whereabouts. House Speaker Mike Johnson and GOP aides admitted they have limited information on Kean's condition. Kean Sr., the former New Jersey governor, stated his son is recovering from a 'serious but temporary illness,' while Kean's chief of staff declined to provide details, saying only 'there's no cameras where Tom is.' Dem

item.title: Behind the Scenes of the Beach Boys’ Pet Sounds Album Cover at San Diego Zoo
item.description: The iconic album 'Pet Sounds' by the Beach Boys, released on May 16, 1966, was largely composed, arranged, and produced by Brian Wilson with contributions from lyricist Tony Asher. Recorded between January and April 1966, the album showcased innovative orchestration that blended pop, jazz, exotica, classical, and avant-garde elements, aiming to surpass the Beatles' 'Rubber Soul' and expand Phil Spector's Wall of Sound technique. To visually represent the album's playful and experimental tone, a photo shoot for the cover was held on February 15, 1966, at the San Diego Zoo. Capitol Records photographer George Jerman captured the band members—Brian, Carl, Dennis Wilson, Mike Love, and Al Jardine—interacting with goats and other animals in the zoo's petting paddock, linking the literal 'pet' concept to the album's title. Contemporary accounts describe a lively, chaotic day filled with laughter and animal antics, including giraffes and llamas. Outtakes from the session feature additional im

item.title: Pete Hegseth Supports Trump-Endorsed Candidate Amid Speculation of 2028 Presidential Run
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s recent appearance on the campaign trail in Kentucky has sparked speculation about a possible 2028 presidential run. Hegseth attended an event supporting Ed Gallrein, a former Navy SEAL running against Republican Congressman Thomas Massie, who had previously endorsed Ron DeSantis in the 2024 primary. Massie criticized the Trump administration for intervening in his race, suggesting that heavy financial backing and campaign involvement indicate desperation. Analysts, including Associated Press reporter Michelle Price, note that Hegseth's participation may serve as a test of his political viability and campaign style, potentially hinting at future ambitions beyond his current role as Secretary of Defense. This development occurs amid declining approval ratings for President Trump, influenced by ongoing challenges such as the Iran conflict and rising living costs. While Massie frames the intervention as an aggressive political move, some observers interpret

item.title: Rodrigo Koraicho Captures the Vibrant Humanity of Miami Beach Through Street Photography
item.description: Brazilian photographer Rodrigo Koraicho has spent years walking along Miami Beach, capturing the ebb and flow of human life under the Florida sun. In his project, 'Beach Epiphany,' he explores the beach as a microcosm of society, documenting moments that range from joyful and intimate to absurd and alienating. Koraicho's approach emphasizes observation and interaction, allowing spontaneous encounters with beachgoers to shape his photographic narrative. His work highlights the diversity of Miami, reflecting cultural identity, economic disparity, and the spectacle of everyday life. From children tumbling in waves to adults performing, flirting, or simply relaxing, Koraicho captures these interactions with a sharp eye for detail, revealing the beauty, desire, and contradictions that coexist in this coastal space. He describes the beach as a stage where vulnerability, boldness, and human impulses are laid bare. By combining patient observation with a sensitivity to composition and color, K

item.title: Controversy Over DOJ $1.776 Billion Fund to Settle Trump Allies' Claims
item.description: The Department of Justice has announced a $1.776 billion fund intended to settle claims from former President Donald Trump, his family, and associates who argue they were politically targeted by previous administrations. This fund, largely funded by taxpayers, comes as part of a settlement in which Trump, his sons, and the Trump Organization agreed to drop a $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS. Critics across social media and political circles have condemned the fund as unfair, citing concerns over its lack of transparency and oversight. Observers note that while some payments may go to individuals involved in the January 6 Capitol attack, much of the money is likely to benefit wealthy Trump allies who claim persecution. Legal and political experts argue that this could set a troubling precedent for using federal funds to settle politically motivated claims. Calls for congressional oversight and potential efforts to reclaim the funds have emerged, highlighting public concern about the

item.title: Behind the Scenes with Joan Collins on the Set of Seven Thieves (1960)
item.description: Released in 1960, 'Seven Thieves' is a classic heist noir film directed by Henry Hathaway, showcasing Edward G. Robinson as an aging criminal mastermind who organizes a team to pull off an ambitious robbery at Monte Carlo Casino. The movie is renowned for its careful pacing, tense atmosphere, and psychologically-driven storytelling, distinguishing it from modern action-heavy films. Joan Collins stars as a seductive dancer whose role is crucial in distracting others during the heist, while Rod Steiger plays the cynical son, adding depth to the ensemble cast. The film’s black-and-white cinematography enhances the tension, and the heist sequence itself is a hallmark of 1960s cinema, emphasizing meticulous planning, silent coordination, and the looming risk that human error could unravel even the perfect plan. Beyond its suspenseful plot, 'Seven Thieves' captures the contrast between the glamorous French Riviera and the desperation of men seeking one last score. These vintage portraits of

item.title: Trump Targets Thomas Massie in Kentucky Primary Over Spending, War, and Epstein Files
item.description: President Donald Trump has intensified his campaign against Republican Representative Thomas Massie, urging his followers to vote Massie out in the Kentucky primary. Massie, despite a history of conservative stances, has opposed several Trump-backed initiatives, including the 'Big Beautiful Bill' and U.S. involvement in the Israel-Iran conflict. The disagreement has extended to the release of government files related to Jeffrey Epstein, with Massie leading efforts alongside Rep. Ro Khanna to make the documents public. Trump has labeled Massie an 'obstructionist and a fool' on social media, while supporting Ed Gallrein, a former Navy SEAL, as a challenger. Gallrein has significantly less political experience and has declined Massie's requests for a debate. This primary will test whether Trump's endorsement and heavy financial support from aligned super PACs can unseat an incumbent in a solidly Republican district. Despite the high stakes and intense campaigning, polls suggest Massie has

item.title: Former GOP Strategist Highlights Republican Party's Ongoing Struggle with Trump Loyalty
item.description: Rick Wilson, co-founder of The Lincoln Project and former Republican strategist, highlights a fundamental flaw within the GOP: the party’s inability to reconcile principle with loyalty to Donald Trump. Wilson points to Senator Bill Cassidy's political experience as an illustrative case. Cassidy voted to impeach Trump during his first administration, then spent years attempting to regain favor by compromising his principles, including voting to confirm Trump-aligned cabinet members. According to Wilson, these efforts were futile because Trump’s loyalty demands are absolute and cannot be negotiated or appeased. This behavior, Wilson argues, represents the GOP's 'terminal blind spot'—a persistent misunderstanding among party members who believe they can balance integrity with allegiance to Trump. Wilson emphasizes that any perceived compromise or attempts at reconciliation are ineffective, as Trump views prior opposition as a permanent betrayal. The article sheds light on the ongoing tens

item.title: The Life and Work of Model Sandy Brown in 1950s Fashion
item.description: Sandy Brown was a highly influential fashion model during the 1950s and 1960s, celebrated for her elegance, poise, and classic features. She became a prominent face in leading fashion magazines such as Life, Vogue, and Harper's Bazaar, as well as appearing in numerous advertisements. Throughout her career, Brown collaborated with some of the era's most renowned photographers, including Lillian Bassman, Milton Greene, Richard Avedon, and Nina Leen, helping shape the visual identity of vintage couture. Her modeling repertoire included a wide range of styles, from intricate evening gowns to stylish daywear, showcasing her versatility and confidence. Iconic images capture her in works by designers such as Elsa Schiaparelli, Anne Fogarty, Carolyn Schnurer, and many others, often accompanied by luxurious accessories and meticulously styled hair and makeup. These photographs not only highlight Brown’s timeless beauty but also offer a window into the fashion and cultural aesthetics of the post

item.title: CAR-T Cell Therapy Shows Early Promise in Long-Term HIV Control
item.description: A small clinical trial has demonstrated that CAR-T cell therapy, a treatment previously used for certain cancers, could help control HIV long-term. In the study, researchers reprogrammed participants' immune cells to specifically target HIV. Two participants achieved undetectable levels of the virus, maintaining viral suppression for nearly two years and almost a year, respectively, without the need for ongoing antiretroviral therapy. The trial involved nine participants, some of whom did not respond as well, highlighting the importance of early treatment and proper conditioning for the CAR-T cells. This approach aims to create 'sentinel' immune cells that remain active in the body to prevent HIV replication. Unlike prior HIV functional cures, which relied on stem cell transplants from rare donors and were complex and risky, CAR-T therapy could eventually provide a safer and more scalable alternative. Challenges remain, including the high cost and labor-intensive preparation of CAR-T c

item.title: Exploring Algal Blooms in the Baltic Sea Through Art and Science
